input_text: Categories: Seafood, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, Farmers Market, Restaurants, Food | Review: We ordered seafood to be delivered on ice and just got it this morning.  1 pound of ahi tuna and 1pound of Scottish salmon. It arrived on ice in a container at the front door. They do a great job shipping overnight!  Tuna looks a bit stringy on one side but we can slice that off later.

Categories: Breweries, Nightlife, Food, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Restaurants, Brewpubs, Gastropubs | Review: I really like the service, and the atmosphere  here great and fresh food drinks are so good i got the
 whiskey  smash sooo good

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: WOW  GOOD TIMES easy to find

we did have to wait 45 minutes for a patio window seat.  but it was worth it,  as the sun set we had after dinner cocktails (almost perfect)  the almost part was that you can not see the sun actually set over the ocean due to this humung rock.  
-
OK BACK TO THE MEAL.  The mrs. had the filet, which was perfect.  My Surf and Turf portion was great for 44.00  nice size lobster and a tasty steak to boot.  there weere plenty of out door heaters to fend of the cool sea breeze.  After our meal we took a nice walk on the beach.  Cant wait to get back!!!   casual dress should be more formal  but we dressed to the 9's

Categories: Dance Clubs, Nightlife, Bars | Review: This is another really fun place to go and have fun.  We really enjoyed the Drag Show that we went to and the dance floor is a great size.  The drinks are pretty stiff so it doesn't take many to get us feeling a little more relaxed.  

I truly enjoyed hanging out on the back patio but the only down-side is the endless smell of smoke for the non-smoker....so it's not 'fresh air' on the patio---it's smoke filled air.  

All in all, it's a fun place to go!

Yvette L. Brownstone from Ventura, California

Categories: Lounges, Bars, American (New), Nightlife, Modern European, Restaurants | Review: Went there during happy hour and ordered one of each happy hour item. Two drinks it was all good not amazing but fun. The device was fantastic!

Categories: Bakeries, Cafes, Coffee & Tea, Food, Desserts, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants | Review: I ordered a cake for a small wedding and we absolutely loved it! My husband loves red velvet so I ordered a 9" round with rustic cream cheese frosting. It was perfect.... my florist provided some flowers so I was able to decorate the cake easily. Next time I'm in Santa Barbara we will make sure to visit. Thank you ...

Categories: Food, Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood, Desserts | Review: Nope! I ordered 4 chile relleno burritos, we got 4 pork burritos.  It was either Chile verde, but there was no sauce, or carnitas. They were very bland, no onions, no cilantro,  This was our first visit and really disappointed when we got home 45 minutes later when we were really looking forward to relleno burritos

Categories: American (New), Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Remarkably fresh, upscale, and delicious. The best Mexican food experience I have ever had. Everything was on point, even the salsas and sides (delectably light salads, rice with queso fresco and mini bean tostadas). I want to go back and try EVERYTHING. They also have a ton of specials that are not listed on their regular menu in addition to their exquisite and ethnic seafood and spins on traditional Mexican dishes like sopes and alambres. Please extend to LA to make my task of eating everything in your restaurant more convenient.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: The pork adobado tacos are out of this world!  Service is friendly, but don't expect them to bend over backwards for you. It's a basic taco joint serving up great food, what more do you need?

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: Amazing sushi! Everything is fresh and delicious. The rolls are very inventive with combination of flavors that really work together as well as look amazing. The only real problem is there are so many enticing items on the menu it is hard to choose. The service was very prompt and friendly. Can't wait to get back here on our next visit to Santa Barbara!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: Amazing service nice staff.  Owner is chef he is very nice and friendly.  Instantly felt welcomed when I came in.  Food is amazing my parents had fish and chips with coleslaw they said it was best they had. I had the haggis it was very good. The haggis reminded me of Scotland haggis there.  The portions are generous so come hungry.  I will definitely come back next time I come to California

Categories: Bubble Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food | Review: Being tourists on a road trip we are not familair with the place so after going to Mission Santa Barbara. We wanted to get some boba as we drove back to Avila beach. After following the 2 GPS which made us walk around Paseo Nuevo we finally found the boba store. The place's deco-approach is like an art gallery style as work from local artisans surround the interior walls. It is colorful and kitchy but full of personality. They have a huge black board wall where the menu is written they can give recommendations too. 

As for their boba, it wasn't much to be excited about. The pearl is still tough in the middle and tea was weak for my taste. I got the house special so I thought it would be their signature drink. I but turned out to be a slight miss. 

Still come for the visual and instagram worthy photo op.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: Nice atmosphere and decently priced, but the food was sub par.  The salsa for the chips was so watery  and bland and probably the some of the worst salsa I've ever had but I was so hungry that I ate it.  

Margarita was so so, and the waitress mixed up which beans went with what plate at our table.

Also this place needs some Mexican music or a Mariachi band playing.

Next time I'm in SB I won't be coming here.
Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: So very delicious for "fast" Italian food.  One of my favorites for many years.  My favorite is the Eggplant Parmesan & Pasta Primavera.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: A little disappointed to be honest. I LOVE my English food, being English and a northern girl, I like the proper comfort food. 

Now, I only indulged in the chicken and mushroom pie w/ chips and mushy peas wrapped in paper (which was awesome btw), the smell was unreal. However, the food itself, not great. I would typically spend $15 in England, get a large bag of chips, chicken and mushroom pie with a pot of gravy and this would not only fill me up, but satisfy my cravings no end. Unfortunately, this wasn't the case. I am pretty sure their bangers and mash and sausage rolls are great but my chips were greasy, too greasy, not enjoyable greasy. My chicken and mushroom pie was toooooooo Americanized. I like it to taste peppery and please don't use chicken breast, it was so dry it was like eating flour! My husband ordered "The Whale". He was excited all day. He wasn't impressed. Him being American, I now have to convince him that English food in England doesn't always taste that bad. The grease was dripping off his fish. I know when you go to a place like this, you should expect a little grease but not so it's causing a puddle in your chippy chip chips. 

Nice place located in a good area, and I am sure if I had gone earlier in the day, I would have had a better and fresher meal. However, when you spend almost $60 on 2 meals and 2 drinks, you expect it to be a little less disappointed. Sorry State Street...

Categories: Pubs, Bars, Food, American (Traditional), American (New), Nightlife, Breweries, Brewpubs, Restaurants | Review: It's amazing place , my RB burger was cooked just like I wanted it and the kombucha was excellent, Nice outdoor sitting it's just a great place

Categories: Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), Event Planning & Services, Bars, American (New) | Review: The endless mimosas here are amazing! They have a yelp deal and that's always a bonus. We were seated promptly for brunch! The crab cakes are apparently amazing.

I had a salad and unfortunately it was not good. It had grilled veggies that were not cooked right and were cold and there was little to no flavor, so I just thoroughly enjoyed the bottomless mims... 

Our sons didn't eat much of their dishes, but I appreciated the layout they came in and the kids menus for them to enjoy. I can't recall what my husband had but I do remember he also enjoyed it a lot! 

Seafood was good and fresh, salad was not. Drinks were abundant!

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Desserts, Wine Bars, Bars, Italian, Nightlife | Review: Poor service 
Slow service 
Noisy 
Very small portions 
Extremely overpriced
Mediocre food
Upside....we didn't get sick

Categories: American (New), Bars, Restaurants, Lounges, Nightlife | Review: Great atmosphere. Excellent service.  Limited wine by the glass menu.  My grey goose martini was watered down.  Grilled Chicken entree was not very good.  Two people in our group ordered it and did not like it.  Spaghetti/lobster was good but a very small portion.  Overall, disappointing.

Categories: Caterers, Italian, Food Delivery Services, American (New), Event Planning & Services, Restaurants, Food | Review: California Pasta is super simple, and quite amazing. Located at this outdoor mall area, and can be missed if you don't deviate from the main street. While it may not look like they get busy, they do. People were coming and going while I was eating, and all the tables just outside of the door were filled. It may not look like a fancy place, but the food is great. This is where you go when you want great quality food for a good price, and as quickly as possible. They're quite efficient and we had our food in front of us in no time.

I had the Chipotle Chicken Panini, which comes with a side of pasta salad. While the panini was delicious, I advise against the Pasta Salad. There's little to no flavor at all. I don't remember if there is an alternative to the pasta salad.

Categories: Sushi Bars, Caterers, Food, Fast Food, Event Planning & Services, Japanese, Food Delivery Services, Korean, Restaurants, Specialty Food, Asian Fusion | Review: Had to give it a 5 star review to level out all the one star reviews. If there wasn't so many one star reviews I woulda gave them a 4-star. The only sushi place in Isla Vista that I know of. They rolls are really good, they miso is super dank, and there staff is really friendly. Your order does take a lil time to make but they also deliver.

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: We probably come here twice a year for a girls night.  The coconut mojito is delicious but all of the other drinks I've tried have been pretty average.  The decor is really cute and it's a nice spot for a drink with the girls.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: It's great! I like that I get to choose what I want in the bowl, and there are lots of choices. Nowadays, they run out of some fish and other choices. I always full from here, even with a regular.  Coming from the Bay Area, I wish that Sno-Crave has toasts and more choices like their other places.

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breweries, American (Traditional), Brewpubs | Review: Three stars for mixed experiences. Pizza's are great, every beer we've tried has been good (we order pitchers not samplers so we've only had a few). One time the boy found a twist tie in his food (one star) and another time we chowed down on the mussels app (four stars). Bottom line, a good, noisy place to get a pitcher and food is generally ok. Oh yeah, and the duck fat fries are an overrated novelty.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New) | Review: I love the atmosphere, decor, and discreet location of Roy.  I also was intrigued that they used fresh local ingredients.  However, after we made reservations for Valentine's day, they did not inform us that they would be using a special menu ONLY.  We simply wanted drinks and dessert.  They escorted us from our seats to the overcrowded bar where we had to wait for stools to clear up.  When we sat down, the bartender refused to serve us dessert, not to mention it took at least 30 minutes to get his attention.  We had to get him to talk to his boss, who finally consented.  What a hassle!  Our romantic evening was ruined by the rude and slow service.

Categories: Soul Food, Wine Tasting Room, Food, Restaurants, Arts & Entertainment, Vegan, Mexican, American (Traditional), Nightlife, Wineries, Bars | Review: Flavor profile on the crab cakes was decent, but quite disappointed in the size. 16$ for two minuscule cakes. (See connect 4 game-piece for reference) I know this place is way overpriced but this is just getting out of hand!

Categories: Indian, Restaurants | Review: Such good Indian food. We came here for the lunch buffet and it was incredibly inexpensive for the great quality. All of us enjoyed it immensely. Lots of Indian folks here, which is always a good sign of authentic cuisine. Will be back for sure.
Categories: Food, Restaurants, Delis, Event Planning & Services, Caterers, Sandwiches | Review: Cute little sandwich shop with great service and excellent sandwiches.

Categories: Delis, Nightlife, Bars, Food, Wine Bars, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Sandwiches, Wineries, Specialty Food, Arts & Entertainment, Cheese Shops | Review: Great sandwiches and selection of gourmet cheeses.  They cut the meats to order very thin for the most flavor and best taste.  Huge selection of fresh lunch meats from peppered turkey to beef tongue.  They have liverwurst and unusual choices and combinations of meats and cheeses not found elsewhere.

All sandwiches are made butcher shop style on sliced bread of your choice with the topping options limited to sweet or dill pickles, mayo, mustard (dijon or regular), pepperoncini, jalapenos and (atomic hot) horseradish sauce for the roast beef.   They have fresh whole pickles and will slice them thin in the meat slicer for unbelievable added flavor and texture to your sandwich.

This place is the bomb and great service from long time employees Kelly and Victoria.  Do not come expecting Subway style with a bunch of veggies to choose dropped on top of pre-weighed and bland lunch meats or you expectations will not be met.  

The quality of the ingredients is top notch and there is not need for the extras.  Simple and honest sandwiches no frills or shortcuts.  Great drink and chip selection to go with the sandwiches.  They also offer catering of meat and cheese trays that are great for most any event and look very impressive.  I am a neighbor and a very frequent customer that just can't stay away.

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: The grilled shrimp taco, seafood soup, and seared ahi burger were all excellent. Some of the best fresh seafood I had since I visited Japan.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Lounges, Seafood, Spanish, Italian, Nightlife, Mediterranean | Review: Found on yelp. Walked 1/2 mile to get there. Half full restaurant, but we were told the kitchen was closed. 8:45 on a Friday night? Yelp says it's open til 10:00pm & late night til 1:30am. Fail.

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: Gorgeous restaurant. I went there with friends during a bachelorette weekend in Santa Barbara. We sat outside around one of the fire pits. It was a cold night but the lovely staff passed around snuggies to everyone who wanted one. The belly dancers were entertaining, the food was delicious and the drinks were on point. Overall we had a wonderful night and I would definitely go back if I were in the Santa Barbara area.

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Nightlife, Restaurants, Seafood, Cafes | Review: So I had the worst Manhattan I've ever had at a bar. And it only cost $16. They really need to up their game to even be as good as the old Paradise. But they are new so let's hope they do that.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Small but great taco spot. They really load up on the protein proportions. So much that I could barely fit any cilantro onions and salsa on top. I ordered chicken beef and pork. The chicken is fajita style chicken which was okay but not a traditional chicken taco that you'd expect. The pork and beef were great though.. Definitely would try again if I was in the area. Super fast service too

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: 3.5

Overall a good dining experience. They only take reservations for the first seating at 5:30pm. Thereafter, it is first come first serve. 

OYSTERS PALACE - Not sure if this is "unlike any you've ever had." I was expecting more jalapeno flavor, so that was disappointing. It tasted very creamy and like a seafood dynamite you would have at an all-you-can-eat buffet. Wouldn't order again. 

CRAWFISH POPCORN -  If you like crawfish, then this appetizer will make your tastebuds happy. It was pretty good and the sauce is savory. Enough to share between 2-3 people. 

REDFISH, which is comparable to snapper fish. It was good and I really enjoyed the freshness of the fish. I have come to believe that cajun food is pretty much dousing your dish in seasoning. With that said, my fish was good and moist, but the flavoring wasn't spectacular. 

JAMBALAYA was good, but didn't leave me wanting more and more.

I tried the Cajun Martini and boy will I never order that again. It is exactly what the description states, chilled vodka with a kick of pepper. You'll probably have enough with just the pint. I really like the cherry peppers that came with the martini, I asked for extra and just chomped them with my Redfish. Not spicy at all. 


Portions here are perfect, not too big. Very good service. Honored our coupons (2) for our party of 10 for free bread pudding! Oh about that, it tastes like a cinnamon.

Categories: Korean, Restaurants, American (New), Mexican, Tacos, Nightlife, Fast Food, Bars | Review: Quick fish taco stop and pow! The explosion of flavor was over the top. We had the triple play + 1 taco. Ahi poke, grilled and batter cod. Awesome!

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (New), Food | Review: Don't begin to understand how they can be rated this highly unless they are "stuffing the ballot box" as they say. Very very disappointing. Expensive breakfast...let me rephrase that, VERY expensive breakfast with deep fried bacon. Yes, you read that right. They deep fried their bacon. It was horrible and tasted horrible. I didn't even finish it. Eggs were cooked wrong. And you're right next to the freeway. I'd give it one star but the waiter was decent.
Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: Favorite club in SB! Plays a variety of danceable music and the staff is really friendly!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Amazing ocean views, right on the beach.  Food is decent and well priced for the area.  Drinks were great and liberally poured.  Fun to watch the dogs off leash just few feet up the beach art he off leash area.  Great place to hang for a few hours.

Categories: Pizza, Gluten-Free, Salad, Restaurants, Fast Food | Review: This is an updated review from my previous post. Given that I had terrible service and food my first couple of times there, I was messaged by a manager who offered me free pizza passes to give them another shot. I was incredibly surprised and pleased to see that they care about their customer experiences. With a free pizza incentive, I had to give them another shot. This time I went in on a Wednesday and during lunch time. I was very pleased with both my pizza and the customer service. It definitely makes a difference when you dine in rather than take it home. There was an employee busting tables and making sure everyone was doing ok. I can say now that I would come back IF (and only if) I decide to dine there during the day. Seems like the employees are tired and annoyed by the late night hours, hence my not so great first visits. Thanks, Blaze!

Categories: Restaurants, Indian, Pakistani | Review: After reading the other reviews we were afraid to try this restaurant, but we were hungry and this was the first Indian restaurant we saw while walking State Street looking for a place for dinner.

First, the service was good compared to other Indian restaurants. There are few Indian restaurants where you will get quality service, and the two waiters that served us here were good about answering our gluten questions and bringing us drinks and poppers in a timely manner. Not outstanding, but we didn't have any issues or complaints. 

We ordered the dal of the day and paneer makhani. We asked for the dal to have a lot of heat and the paneer to have a medium heat. The dal had a lot of heat (not all restaurants can make a spicy dal) and tasted as if it could have been made by one of our grandmothers. The paneer was good, but it had no heat and was too sweet, although it was still edible. The naan and poppers were good.

We came by the next afternoon to look at the buffet, and after reading the menu posted in the window never bothered to enter the restaurant as the vegetarian options were small and not exciting at all, especially at the listed price. 

We would eat here the next time we are in town, but will only order off the menu. I am always really excited about a restaurant that doesn't make my celiac hubby sick, and there were no issues with him eating here.

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Latin American, Mexican, Seafood | Review: Can't get myself to eat any other Mexican food in town right now. This place is so good and has really good prices. I don't review much but felt the need because they deserve it.

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), French, Restaurants | Review: Such a lovely place. The food is excellent and reasonably priced. We enjoyed a drink by the fire before getting a nice table for two in the house part of the restaurant (as opposed to the greenhouse). Our waiter, Martin, was great and he offered very helpful suggestions on the menu and wine list. I can whole heartedly recommend the escargot, mussels, and the duck burger. Delicious. And their espresso macchiato was well balanced. It would have been better in a demitasse, but it's not yet a perfect world. Will definitely go back. A new favorite near Montecito.

Categories: Seafood, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, Farmers Market, Restaurants, Food | Review: Was disappointed when I got here. I was expecting an actual fish market but once in, there were only two displays of seafood in a small room where you order from the counter.

On top of that, they were out of the Uni since the day before and had no idea when their next shipment would come in.

I guess my expectations were just high but it seems like prices were average. I'll be back if I'm ever in the area for their uni!

Categories: American (Traditional), American (New), Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Seafood, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Venues & Event Spaces | Review: Horrible margarita! So heavy not refreshing at all which sucks because that is what our waiter suggested other than a Pims which from past experiences I know is not a light refreshing drink either. Very disappointed

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Cafes, Cajun/Creole | Review: I only go to the one on Calle Real. 
Dad and I are frequent Flyers, so we've become friends with most of the staff.
I enjoy the food. Portions are large enough to share. Love the grits! It's a southern thing. I'd definitely recommend.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: Spot on service excellent food great design inside had paella queso pan con tomate horchata

Categories: Vietnamese, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, Wine Bars, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Noodles | Review: I have no clue why this place has the low review it does. It was excellent! Super fresh ingredients and lovely service. I love Pho and come from an asian family so I know good Vietnamese food when i have it. Such a romantic atmosphere outside as well. They have the most beautiful outdoor setup i've seen on state street. We'll definitely be back soon!
Categories: Breweries, Restaurants, Sports Bars, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Food, Bars | Review: Sorry bad

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: HARBOR RESTAURANT:  I have to say I had one of the worst meals I have had in many years two nights ago at the Harbor Restaurant.    The manager Jordan was very unprofessional and did not seem to care a bit.

I had the Bacon Wrapped Diver Scallops with Mushroom Risotto and asparagus.  The Risotto was under-cooked and under seasoned and COLD.  The rest of the dinner I managed to get down but this is not what you would expect from a restaurant like this.  The chef must not have tasted anything he put on that plate.  

At this point I am not willing to give this place anymore of my money.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: Excellent fish and chips!  As authentic as it gets and pretty darn close to what I had in Broadstairs (seaside) England.  

my favorite things:
The tartar sauce was AMAZING.  
Mushy peas were SOOO tasty!  
Haggis was delicious.  

Next time I drive through SB, I must stop by and try other things on the menu.

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: Bottom Line; a beautiful place to take in a beautiful meal

     My folks and I came here for dinner one beautiful night, and sat outside and drank in the evening air.  We ordered the huge sampler plate and moved bites of food around the table trying to soak up as much of it as we could.
     Places like this are such a lovely antidote to the corporate eatery.  C'mon - come on in, sit down, order something you cant pronounce, share it with everyone at the table..... actually live your life to some of its potential during a meal for once....

     This is a really cool spot worth checking out for sure.  Sit outside and bask in it

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, American (Traditional), Venues & Event Spaces | Review: Love this place they have he best bread and salsa stake bites ,spicy tots, and beef dips ,and fish and chips you have to try it.
Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I love, love, love this place. When you're in the mood for great Mexican food this is a must!!!

Categories: Barbeque, Golf, Active Life, Restaurants | Review: I got to play the course a number of years ago.  Then they shut it down for a couple of years.  This past Friday I got a chance to go back. 

This course is beautiful, challenging, and has some of the best greens!!  

One other nice upgrade is they now allow beer/wine at the course.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: The ambience is great.  Eat outside; too loud inside to have a conversation.  Menu is meant to be shared.  We ordered crispy brussell sprouts & deviled eggs to start.  Brussels were roasted and lovely.  Even folks who don't like them in general like them.  The eggs were ok, but just ok.  We ordered roasted beets, the wedge and roasted carrots from the "salad"/farm section.  All were stellar.  For our mains we had crispy lamb and pappardelle and the hanger steak.  Steak was awesome.  The lamb I felt was more over done than crispy but the noodles were yummy.  Service was stellar.  My only complaint was the timing of the dishes; one second your table is jammed but then we only had the carrots... Learn to pace things in a logical way.  Two thumbs up with free valet parking.

Categories: Restaurants, Thai | Review: Awesome food. We were here on a Thursday night and ordered the spicy fried noodles.  I really like hot food, and this hit the spot.  Not too hot, but I added extra heat to it to meet the spice level I require.  Great location by the beach, within walking distance.  Outdoor seating is nice.  A must visit for Thai lovers.

Categories: Food, Vietnamese, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: Quick, inexpensive, authentic Vietnamese. Food comes out in minutes. My cousin and I shared an egg noodle pho with dumplings, chicken with mixed veggies, and egg rolls. We paid $25 including tip and had leftovers. There is also a nice assortment of condiments on every table. Best Vietnamese in SB

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, French, Salad, Sandwiches, Desserts, Bakeries, Food | Review: Awesome French bakery! This place was perfect for breakfast and is in a little shopping plaza with some great stores if you have to wait for a table. You'll want to plan on taking extra pastries home :)

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: My family made a reservation at The Lark for 14 people to celebrate my graduation from UCSB. The whole process was thoughtful and enjoyable, from the emails exchanged between the planner and my mom (discussing menus and portions) to the 5-star table service we received. Our waitress was on top of everyone's dietary restrictions (dairy, spices, tree nuts, and poultry - we're a fun bunch, I know), and the chef had no problem accommodating these restrictions. The atmosphere is always so lovely, and of course, the food is artisan quality and beautifully prepared. However, what I want to focus on in this review is how impressed we were with the service we received and how special it was to have such a nice celebration the night of my graduation. I will miss The Lark and Lucky Penny and will go out of my way to stop by when visiting Santa Barbara.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Good not great - mole negro was very one note mostly semi sweet chocolate not the depth I was hoping for, drinks decent, sounded a bit better than they were, the fundido was good but darn greasy - kind of the name ire of the beast but to its detriment- salsas and chips excellent, service great

Categories: Restaurants, Nightlife, Mexican, Bars, Cocktail Bars | Review: 4 of us went to happy hour yesterday at Flor De Maiz. We were waited on by Miguel who cheerfully explained all the offerings at the new restaurant. We ordered everything off the happy hour menu and quite a few margaritas! Yummy and fresh! Fantastic service. We will be back. Thank you Miguel!!!

Categories: Restaurants, Burgers, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch, Diners | Review: The place looks cool in a Hawiien theme like, it was clean an the food was good.  A bit pricy but overall I'll give it 6 out of 10

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: I should preface this review by saying that I've traveled across the world in search of tasty food, I don't like white washed, Americanized renditions of dishes. 

If you want a place, that isn't authentic or flavorful then I would highly advise you to try this establishment. Why is it so highly rated? One factor that it has going for it is its location. The location within this bouje food court is impeccable, and from an aesthetic view point it has everything going for it. But when I order medium spicy and I can't taste the spice in either the soup or the noodles, I ask myself why did I even come to a thai restaurant? The boat noddle soup broth is so mediocre I'm shocked by the crowd that lines up for this place.

Prices are also high, for the experience and the service. I would recommend staying away from this bland establishment. 

One caveat I should add is that perhaps my experience was on an off day, but when you are tourist you only have one opportunity to taste something I would take this place off your list of need to try restaurants in SB.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: Let's start off with a spicy blood orange margarita please!! We ordered a ceviche sampler that was ok to me because I make ceviche at home and it was lacking a little lime juice flavor but the mango pico de gallo that was mixed in there was delicious. My boyfriend had the chicken tortilla soup which was very hearty and filled with chunks of chicken. I had the wild blackened salmon salad which was incredible! It had slivers of mango and bell pepper and the salmon was perfectly cooked. The crispy texture of the fish with a light bed of greens and a bite of the sweet slightly tart mango all in one bite was amazing. I had to finish this meal off with a shot of Jameson!! Highly recommend this warm cozy restaurant. There were many flat screens, everyone seemed like they were having a great time in there. The waitress was also very nice!
Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Coffee Roasteries | Review: Well roasted beans. Very basic flavors, not very dynamic. Neat vibes in store. Very quiet staff. We would go again.

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: This place definitely can not handle the theater rush.  The food took 45 mins to come out.  For a two person party, the first meal, chicken teriyaki, came out cold and tough, on a bed of shredded cabbage. the sauce was watery and fairly tasteless.  A little chili thrown in for spice is all that was noticeable aside from how crappy it was.  15 mins later, the mush they called Pad Thai came out for the 2nd person in the party.  It was way overpriced for the bad quality and service.  The staff was friendly BUT sorry, that's the ONLY reason I give it two stars.  We won't be back.  McDonald's (and I can't believe I'm saying this) would have been better and it was just across the street.  Very disappointed.  Been to many Japanese and Thai restaurants and have NEVER had such horrible food.  Not even close.  Chef Ramsey should be sent to this place for a Kitchen Nightmares episode. They truly need help!

Categories: Chinese, Restaurants | Review: I had really high expectations because of the rating this "restaurant" had on google but it was extremely disappointing. Our server was accommodating, but the food was the problem. The fried shrimp was tasteless, even with the sauce that it came with. The wontons were burnt and stale (my sister thought they were...something else). The almond chicken had a strong pee odor and there were random bits of what seemed like bone. The kung pao was bland and had a slight bitter taste to it, and was a joke to label it "spicy" when it barely had any taste. For the poor quality of the food it was incredibly over priced, and the lemonade (from a regular soda can) had more flavor than the whole meal put together. Just getting plain rice at Panda Express would of been a much better option in this situation.
Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Great Mexican food. The chips were fresh and hot. Service was quick and our waitress was extremely friendly. I would eat their carnitas everyday. Yum.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: I've been here twice now and this is one of my favorite upscale Mexican restraunts in town. Indoors it's crowded so if possible, sit on the outdoor patio. It gets VERY crowded on the weekends so expect to wait at least 30 min to an hour or longer depending on your party size. The hostess was the same both times and she was super sweet and patient even when they were really crowded! They have wait times live on Yelp and you can even get on the waitlist from there which is really convenient! You can also give them your phone number to text when your table is ready so at least you can walk around while you wait. And this place is totally worth it. You get chips and variety of salsas which is a major plus. Everything I've tried on the menu is super savory and tasty. The portion sizes are huge so you could definitely share an entree with someone if you're not super hungry. My favorite item is the Cazuela Pibil. Really delicious marinated pork.
Categories: Tex-Mex, Restaurants | Review: Great outdoor patio area.  Loved the ambiance.  The chips and salsa are good, food was okay.  Nothing special.

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Visited Sam's today for lunch and got the #13 (a classic Italian) and it was extremely tasty. Price was excellent, service was super friendly and quick. This place is perfect for a sandwich for the beach or for work. They also do catering of large subs and I'm looking forward to ordering one for my next big party.

Categories: Food, Live/Raw Food, Restaurants, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Asian Fusion, Poke, Seafood, Food Delivery Services | Review: This review is for their poke bowl - I did not try their ice cream. My main issue with this place is how cheap they are - if they had a scale, they would weigh everything they give you down to the gram.

We went in on a Friday night to find the place empty - they were four employees and they were all in the back watching YouTube videos or something. They finally saw us and looked annoyed that we interrupted them. We ordered two bowls, and while I understand the need to charge extra for extra protein (such as fish and etc.), they were trying to charge us extra for everything from a little more rice to a little more edamame...

Once we got the bowls, it tasted okay. However, I would not recommend going here. I understand it is a business, but you can't be THAT cheap.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: The noodle bar is located in the center of Santa Barbara Public Market. Just like all the other places inside the market, the noodle bar gave a very modern, clean, and hipster vibe. We ordered the "Bangkok Street Noodle" and "Long Tail Boat Noodle". The street noodle was pho noodles in spicy and sour soup with bbq pork, minced pork, and fish balls on top. My favorite part of this dish was their bbq pork, because it was very tender. The boat noodle was pho noodles in dark colored soup (couldn't tell what the soup was made of...) with beef and beef meatballs on top. I was not impressed by the boat noodle, and I would recommend that you try other things on their menu.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Absolutely love this place. Their noodle soups are exceptional. Curry soup and Thai street noodle both have my heart. Generous portions  made with fresh ingredients. It is fascinating to observe the chefs as they basically cook in front of you. Come early if you intend to go for lunch, seating is limited.

Categories: Bars, Mediterranean, Restaurants, Nightlife, Moroccan, Event Planning & Services, Venues & Event Spaces, Italian | Review: They don't honor the axxess deal which is "buy one food item, get one free (max value: $20)" We bought a salad, a sandwich, and two drinks which came out to $60. None of the items were more than $20. They said that it was on a minimum of a $20. They wouldn't even give us the 10% of thereafter deal. We're locals so the the only reason we even went was to use the axxess card. To top it off, they're drinks were way too sweet, and their food was alright.
Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: good, interesting, ice cream & a jumping place

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional) | Review: I found the food to be very mediocre coffee shop food, Denny style.   No maple syrup.  Everything is very convenience type food.  The only reason I gave it a star was the people were very friendly, kind and wanted to be of service. #verything was clean. I went to this restaurant based on the reviews. And was extremely disappointed.  I am a big foodie.  If you are not a foodie, maybe you will like it.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New) | Review: 7/21/07. It was a late Saturday night and I was feeling hungry. Since this is my second time in SB I was hoping to go to Bouchon. But they were all booked and their kitchen closed at 10:00pm. It was 10:30pm and I remember hearing of Roys being a great place to dine late night. Just several blocks away from Bouchon we arrived at Roys. It was a really small place and it looked more like a bar spot with fried food. We sat there for awhile and finally was served water. The menu was very very very limited. Approx 10 dishes on the menu and it was a hand written menu. Few minutes more we got our order taken. We ordered the Seabass. That sounded really good. Our ordered came in a timely manner. The seabass was good, but it tasted like something else. Hmmm, what is it? On my second bite, my partner in crime figure it out. This fish tastes like steak. Apparently the chef must have not cleaned the grill and just cooked the fish after he finished cooking someone's steak. It was very disappointing. On top of that our server never came to check on us. When we finally finished our meal she still have not came to check if we need anything else. Was this place busy? Nope not at all. Everyone was just talking and hanging out.  We flagged her down and she finally gave us refills and our check. Since I was hungry and I had a headache I didn't want to complain about the fish. We just finished it quickly to satisfy our hunger. There's nothing spectacular about this place except our soup. I can trurhfully state that my brocolli soup was one of the best I have tasted. Would I try this place again? Since it is a long trip for me to come to SB and if I visit again I will prob never go back to Roys. There's too many other great restaurants to try for me to come back to this place.

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Food was grade A. Service is pleasant. Ambiance is mediocre and their menu is relatively inventive and the drinks are made perfect. Really loud, but not obnoxious. The convenience of adding yourself to the waitlist on yelp is nice feature, but not sure it was worth a long wait. Certainly recommend!

Categories: Japanese, Sushi Bars, Restaurants | Review: Great sushi in a very friendly environment. They have some interesting choices on the menu and the owner is delightful. She is very considerate towards her clients. A "must visit" if you are in the area.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Very nice service, best agave Margarita I've had. Shrimp enchiladas were amazing!
Everything was fresh, tasted wonderful!

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Barbeque, Food, Sandwiches, Wine Bars, Diners, Bars, Specialty Food, American (Traditional), Nightlife | Review: Barbareño is a must try if you're in Santa Barbara! Our favorite dishes included the eggamuffin (a gourmet spin on the McDonald's egg McMuffin, which originated in Santa Barbara) and the wagyu tri tip.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: The food is to die for!!!! And of course beautiful beach views. 

We got a curry mahi mahi dish and it was a litttleeee dry, but I really enjoyed the seafood pasta dish and lobster bisque. I would definitely recommend eating here if you are visiting SB.

Categories: Pizza, Nightlife, Italian, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Maybe good for Santa Barbara standards?  I call this "expensive mediocracy!"  If you have ever lived in Seattle, Portland (Oregon), or New York, you can get this for 30% less and 30% better with similar ambience.  There salads are way too expensive and not worth the disappointment of (as I recall) $12.00.  Should be half the cost for the salad my family and I all had.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Food, American (New), Desserts, Restaurants | Review: What an excellent experience! Our waiter, Bill, was professional, engaging, anticipated our needs, and gave us first class service. The food was excellent and the atmosphere was hip and cozy. We had the risotto which was decadent and delicious. The hamachi crudo was a bright and interesting flavor palate
Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese | Review: Quality fish, great prices, open late. Been coming here for years and don't plan on stopping.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: You won't find a more wretched hive of villainy and scum. Nelson runs the back patio in a way that any Hutt would be envious of. Don't bother trying your Jedi mind tricks on him, they won't work, unless your a hot girl, wearing less clothing than is work appropriate.  Sadly, no Rancor pit (that I noticed). They play good tunes.  Drinks are solid.

Categories: Sandwiches, Salad, Bagels, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, Restaurants | Review: Slightly overpriced food,  typical mediocre carbohydrate fare. Cashier  was angry because I did not tip her for taking my order. Often runs of out of everything-- usually bagels ( sad being it's a bagel shop). Also it closes at 3 which is ridiculous because that's the time everyone wants to wake up in a beach town.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Venues & Event Spaces, Peruvian, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Nightlife, Latin American, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Tapas/Small Plates, Mexican | Review: Hands down the best margarita in Santa Barbara! Perfect balance between sweet and citrus. AND if you go during happy hour when they are $6, well, it doesn't really get much better. 

The food is also really well prepared and presented. During my last visit I ordered ceviche verde and the totopos con mole( which are similar to nachos), and I was happy with both dishes. My only complaint is the chips to dish ratio is a little high. The totopos definitely could have used more beans and mole. On other occasions I've had dishes from their raw bar and usually leave wanting more. 

Service and atmosphere also a plus! The restaurant is in a beautiful courtyard and perfect to sit outside on a nice day, but also really elegant inside for evening visitors.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Latin American, Caterers, Food, Restaurants, Empanadas, Argentine | Review: Stop what you are doing and eat here.  We ordered a family meal - 3 beef empanadas and 3 Chicken Empanadas and an order of Chimi Papas.  The food is so damn good.  Crispy light breading and perfectly seasoned meat. And the service was so warm and friendly.  We got it to go but they have a great outdoor seating area.  Cannot recommend highly enough!

Categories: Vegan, Vegetarian, Restaurants, Indian | Review: Highly disappointing!

My girlfriend and I really like Masala Spice but it's always a guess on what the quality will be from visit to visit. This has happened to us more than twice. In the past, we loved the fish pakora and Saag but the last two times we got them, they were terrible! We had to add some spice or something to it to make it edible. It's not a $16 worth meal at all.

At this point, I don't think we are going to order from this restaurant anytime soon. You might stand a good chance if the chef is in a good mood.

Categories: Restaurants, Barbeque, Mexican | Review: I love Santa Barbara Chicken Ranch, I don't know why I didn't write a review for this place when I was here for graduate school. I always get the 1/4 chicken plate with extra chips which go very well with the delicious salsa available at the Salsa bar. The meat is just smokey enough and very tasty. Simple restaurant where you pay at the register and they bring you the food. Wet naps are available to clean up but sometimes they are dried out.

The only real negative that I found is that the chips are not my favorite. A bit dry in my opinion.

Categories: Food, Bubble Tea, Chinese, Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Hot Pot | Review: Food is good. However, customer service is terrible. We waited for about an hour and still didn't get our food while people who came later than us even finished their meal. Terrible, terrible service. Never seen something like this.

Categories: Restaurants, Seafood, American (New), Steakhouses | Review: Carson remembered my name when I walked in! 

Jmichael & Patricia waiters 
Ramon dish brings
Conner helps get all orders 

Zach is sommelier 

This placce is ace! 

Order soufflé as you seat down. So it can BBEdit well made foR dessert and after youR meals. 

They also has smoke liquor which I wish someone ordered from my ttanle or near.  I wouldn't mind watching getting prepare at the bar and bbeing see reed at the near by table   Problems are I didn't get to taste it! 

Everything I tasted are fabulous  

Come and enjoy the sunset and fine dining. 

Check me out at the bar or cOme seat at the booth. 
See you soon!

Categories: Italian, Restaurants, Pizza | Review: It's amazing this restaurant is still open.  They have a really good beet salad, but the restaurant is almost always empty.  They recently took down an overhead portion above the bar and it's really lightened the place up.  I like the booths which give you the ability to have a private conversation and they're comfortable.  The bread is okay.  They often have coupons in the paper for buy one, get one free and that's what makes it worth coming back for (and I really like the beet salad).

Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: I really enjoyed their mint chip, super tasty and was probably the best mint chip ice cream I've had. 

They only are allowing walk up to the window and order, cards only. Love how they wrap your ice cream cone orders in a napkin with the name on it.

Worth trying when in SB.

Categories: Asian Fusion, Chinese, Restaurants | Review: Buffet Only review: it was terrible. There weren't many options , and it wasn't fresh...at all. The service was also very lacking. We were there for over two hours and no one came by to fill our water. Also, we asked to see the manager and our server told us that the manager was too busy. Not cool, man.

Since we didn't get our money's worth, we asked of anything could get discounted because of how sad the options were, and the waiter just stood there arms crossed saying that wasn't possible.

Also, the booths and tables are sticky , so consider yourself warned.

I'm sure there are bright sides  to this place, but we just weren't fortunate enough to experience them.

Categories: Indian, Restaurants | Review: This was our second time here and it continues to remain one of our favorites.  There are many choices of trendy places to eat in SB, however most of them overcharge and fall short on the food.  Tamira's food tastes great, the service is wonderful, and there are a range of choices.  There is always plenty of seating, and the waiters are very attentive and friendly.
I definitely recommend the lunch buffet, but the dinner menu is also super.  Everything on the menu tastes great!  If you want to just get an introductory taste, definitely check out the garlic naan and the Butter Chicken Masala!
This place is wonderful!

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: I have tried this place a few times when visiting from Los Angeles. Not sure if I came on an off day (considering the number of good reviews here), but the last time I was here, my pad thai was sickeningly sweet. I had to ask for lime juice (no fresh limes here?) to counter the sweetness of the dish and even then, it was too sweet. The price is moderate-high depending on your wallet. Pad thai was $13.95+tax with other dishes within the same range ($13.95-15.95). 

The service is good and there has generally been a wait every time I have been here. If there is not enough seating at the table, you can order it to go and eat it at another table nearby. Items like pad see ew are only served during a specific day of the week along with other noodle specialities.

Categories: Restaurants, American (Traditional), Lounges, Nightlife, Bars, Burgers, American (New) | Review: Update review...
My Daughter and I drove up to Sta Barbara for the day. It's been some time since we've been to this location.
What a lovely experience,  probably because it was a Monday evening. Superb customer service by 2 waitresses! 
We ordered the truffle fries, nachos and 2 burgers, yum! Truffle fries are Always worth it especially when they give a decent side dish of melted cheese. The nachos had a very tasty smokey Chipotle sauce with plenty of chopped chicken and chopped avocado, could've used more black beans. I counted 9 beans. Ha! Our burgers were cooked to perfection. The Napa burger and fig burger are a must to try!
Great experience!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Fish & Chips, British | Review: I tried the fish sandwich and it was good. Nothing extraordinary though. The Fish is fresh,  nicely battered. Solid four stars. My taste buds did not go crazy from the tartar sauce so I cannot elaborate anymore. The restaurant has a very pleasant feel to it. Next time I decide to indulge in fried food I will definitely make this my number one choice and try something else from the menu . 

The food is a bit pricey, but just remember that most restaurants located on State Street are not cheap.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: This place is unbelievable... the drinks... the food... just amazing! 
Great staff as well!

Categories: Restaurants, Ethiopian, French | Review: The menu here may not do the restaurant justice because it seems a bit too limited, but the food is sublime. I had Seabass which is the best I've ever had as well as crabcake which is the best I've had anywhere in the country from Maryland  to California. Even the bread was to die for. And a wonderful Field green salad with beets and goat cheese.   The chef here really must be a genius.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Delis, Breakfast & Brunch, Tapas/Small Plates, Cafes | Review: The fig toast was amazing! The portions were very generous for being small plates and everything was delicious. Would definitely go back next time I'm in town.

Categories: Cajun/Creole, Breakfast & Brunch, Caterers, Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, Burgers | Review: Consistently just as delicious as the one in Ventura. 

Had the Rancher's omelet (cheddar, bacon, tomatoes) n smothered it with cajun seasoning and cholula..yum! Their bacon is very hearty and they don't skimp!

Biscuit with a side of gravy is never a disappointment. Biscuits are massive. However, side of gravy is $2.70, but you get a whole bowl, so split it with a friend. It was fun to sit n eat on the porch.

Categories: Sushi Bars, Szechuan, Cantonese, Restaurants, Chinese | Review: This review is for the sushi bar only, as I have never tried the Chinese food here.
Both the chefs at the sushi bar seem to work every day (except Sunday) and are so incredibly friendly. The ordering system is easy - mark your picks off on a slip, hand to the waiter or directly to the chef. 

They have an amazing dinner deal from 4-6 where most of the sushi is half off, which is the main reason we originally came to Empress Palace. What made us regular customers was the quality of the fish, and the kind service from the sushi chefs. 

The fish is always fresh - I have never had a questionable piece here. I even tried their uni one time (was a little hesitant), but turns out it was wonderful. I especially enjoy the sapporo roll.
Categories: Delis, Restaurants | Review: Greek house blend gyro with salad is literally my favorite lunch. We drive from goleta just to get greek house!

Categories: Health Markets, Coffee & Tea, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Restaurants, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, American (New), Specialty Food | Review: Absolutely delicious, fresh ingredients, gluten free granola. I had the Diego bowl and it's super tasty, sizes are large packed with goodies, textures and flavors. Definitely stop by and grab one. Corner of State and Guitierrez!

Categories: Caterers, Food, Event Planning & Services, Personal Chefs | Review: We had Danny cater our 90 person wedding this weekend in Ojai and our guests raved about the food!  The appetizers were delicious and the dinner fantastic.  Also, his staff did a great job and kept a great attitude despite the terrible heat!  Thanks so much, Chef Danny for making our wedding perfect!

Allyson & Matt Lambert

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Venues & Event Spaces, Peruvian, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Nightlife, Latin American, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Tapas/Small Plates, Mexican | Review: Just a service review: our host was so rude, scatter brained, impatient and seemed very stressed out. Then our server seemed to redeem our first impression because she was so nice and informative. We ordered drinks and we however wanted authentic Mexican food and decided to try a different restaurant. She then seemed agitated and irritated. As a food server myself I believe we are supposed to make the guests experience as enjoyable as possible from beginning to end. This experience was less than pleasant and we won't be returning.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese, Asian Fusion | Review: On our roadtrip down to Santa Barbara, my girlfriend and I wanted to have some Japanese sushi rolls for dinner. Having read the reviews for arigato sushi, we decided try it. 

The restaurant was really packed, but we were fortunate to get seats at the bar. Lots of sushi chefs were at the bar and were working really quick. They also took our orders. 

We ordered the Arigato roll, Dracula roll, premium 7 nigiri sushi and a spicy tuna hand roll.

The rolls were just okay. Nothing different or special about them.  We've paid less for much better rolls.  But the service was really good.  The fish was really fresh. The sushi was tasty.

This place is popular and gets packed really fast.  Recommend making reservations.  If you are a couple, I suggest getting seats at the sushi bar.  The sushi chef in front of you will take care of your orders. And did I mention they are quick?

Return? Likely, if we're in the area.

Categories: Caterers, Restaurants, Spanish, Personal Chefs, Event Planning & Services | Review: Oh my goodness, what a treat!  I hired SB Paella to cater an event and they were so easy to work with and our guests raved about the paella.  I will definitely be hiring them again.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, Salad, Coffee & Tea, Tex-Mex, American (Traditional), Burgers, Food, Sandwiches, Mexican, Juice Bars & Smoothies | Review: Her salad was good, but nothing special my wife said.  I had the pasta special and it was rather bland -- shredded cheese straight from a pack, creamy sauce.  Living with a vegetarian, there are a lot of ways to "make it up" (fresh garlic, fresh herbs, grated cheese, pepper flakes, etc.) -- none of those "tricks" made it in my special.
It might be more a lunch place than dinner -- the ambiance was more cafeteria than dining.  We'll may go back, but for lunch.
As a vegetarian, my wife would rate one star above my 2 because there are a lot choices.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Latin American, Caterers, Food, Restaurants, Empanadas, Argentine | Review: I got the pollo, serrano and carne empanadas and they were all damn good. Would recommend this place if you're in the mood for empanadas

Categories: Gluten-Free, American (New), Restaurants, Sandwiches, Salad, Steakhouses, Delis, American (Traditional) | Review: Awesome Husband loves good pastrami and this was great.  Even cold the next day was great. Roast beef sandwich was fantastic.

Categories: Burgers, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Burger has an unusual flavor. I can't quite put my finger on it, but it has a strange aftertaste. The patty is also EXTREMELY thin, and I ordered a double. Also, not a generous amount of fries given and they lack salt so they're essentially flavorless. I was disappointed, hoping for a pleasant hole-in-the-wall experience. This was not it.

Categories: Bars, American (Traditional), Gastropubs, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: Just passing by, decided to stop and get something to eat. The restaurant seemed nice from the reviews. It was a bit windy, so it was quite uncomfortable to sit outside. We sat right away and the waiter took our order quickly, but our appetizer was cold when it got to our table. I ordered a side a ranch, but it never got to our table. At the end, we waited a long time for the waiter to take our check, we had to take it to the counter. Overall, the food and drinks weren't bad and the staff wasn't rude, but it did seem like they weren't really paying attention. They had more than enough staff to wait on tables.

Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, Italian, Pizza, Restaurants | Review: I'll be frank, the reviews regarding this restaurant must be derived from a parallel universe because they certainly don't prove to be true at Nutbelly Pizzeria & Deli I visited in this reality.

The service was horrible. Menu read well but fell far short on delivery. I ordered a pizza with pepperoni, sausage, bacon and meatballs. You'd think bacon alone would redeem my taste buds but no dice. (How do you screw up bacon?)

The crust was the only attribute on this pie worth a modicum of praise. The meat was tasteless, and the meatballs were more like meat clumps and devoid of flavor and any enjoyable juicy texture.

Overall the pie was far from gourmet. It tasted bland and left my palate disappointed nay, frustrated. If you judged a pizza solely by it's looks it certainly wasn't ugly but beauty in this case is in the mouth of this consumer and Nutbelly was found wanting.

Ordering a glass of wine to go with this pizza was assuming too much in hindsight.

Unfulfilled by the pie I decided to see how they pulled of Tiramisu. It wasn't bad, it just wasn't memorable either and that sums up my entire dining experience at Nutbelly.

Compound the above with the fact that their prices are too high for the level of food they are serving.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Seafood | Review: Called ahead to check the wait and was told 20-25 minutes so we headed that way. Upon arrival we were told 20 minute wait. After 25 minutes of waiting the hostess came up to us and said sorry kitchen is backed up and it would be another 20 minutes. I asked if we could sit at one of the tables (15-20 empty tables) and order margaritas and have some chips and salsa and I was told no! I own two businesses and I would not be happy to hear this is the customer service I they delivered.

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: This is a SB must try!!!  
I highly recommend the KFC Bao. It will really make you say, "WOW!" 
Great fast and friendly service.  And...we wish we knew how to make Bok Choy  taste this good at home.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: We had our company party here last night and everything was excellent! The staff was super friendly, cleaned up after everyone and so polite. The bartenders made some great drinks, I personally had 3 margaritas.. Nice and strong. Would definitely recommend this place for the excellent service we received.

Categories: Japanese, Sushi Bars, Restaurants | Review: This little sushi house in Old Town Goleta is memorable because of the good food, but especially the friendly family that runs it. They treat you like family and keep the sake flowing. 

Try the "golden shiso" appetizer!

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: The open air ambience was nice but the food was just ok. Only the fresh oysters and giant fresh clams were the stars here. Other than that, a nice place to enjoy some drinks and view the harbor.

Two orders of half a dozen oysters (4/5) they were one of the freshest oysters we had so far in Santa Barbara. Pricey however but good


Cioppino (2/5) this was ok. Barely had any seafood to the tomato broth ratio. The tomato flavor tasted like canned tomato. 

Steamed clams (4/5) light and juicy. The clams are big here. 

Overall a 3 in my book. Would be back for shucked oysters and steamed clams

Categories: Delis, Food, Restaurants, Bakeries | Review: I have lived near this bakery for a very long time and I would drive by it daily. Wow, was I in for a surprise. They have an extensive assortment of mexican bread and they make everything from pineapple upside down cake (my fav), brownies, turnovers, conchas and  cookies fresh daily. They have delicious tamales (vegan offerings too!). It can get crowded because the waiting area is a bit small but a smile goes a long way. I try to tell everyone about this Bakery because it's delicious, the prices are right & freshness is always a priority.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: By far, the MOST authentic Mexican food I have ever had in Santa Barbara! This place beats every Mexican place I have been to here. So so delicious! This was also the most swipe ups I got on my snapchat asking what this place was called. So everyone else thought it looked good too;) 

Their food is extremely flavorful. I got the chicken enchiladas mild and the sauce was so tart, tangy, and smooth. My friend got the fish tacos, and she said they were the best fish tacos she has ever had in her life. My friend specifically asked for his food spicy and they asked him if he was sure. Sure enough, he said his food was SUPER spicy. So if you like your food super spicy, ask them to make it hot. 

The service here is also just as amazing! The waiter and waitress attended to us whenever we needed and gave us some great recommendations.

P.S. their soft drinks/ horchata/ jamaica have free refills!! I probably drank at least 3 good cups of jamaica.

Categories: Food, Vietnamese, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: The Pho here is one of the best in town, however the service is one of the worst.

When Noodle City first opened the service was great, however over the time they don't seem to care.

Today I ordered the House Special Pho and the both was good--little fat, good body and a little sweet.  The side of spouts, limes and jalapenos were lacking.   I only got two slices of peppers!

There was only ONE table besides mine and none of us got offered any water or checked on.  I'm glad it wasn't just me...

After I payed for the food the owner didn't even look at me when she said thanks.  She simply said "sign, thanks" while she walked away--just plain sad.

I love the food here but I won't be coming back.  I've been here way too many times  to be treated like this over and over again.  You can't have a restaurant business that doesn't put any effort on service. The whole point on eating out is to chill and not trip on anything.

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: To get a good idea of how far McConnell's has fallen since they were sold several years ago, go to Trader Joe's and buy a large 5 pint container of their super premium vanilla ice cream (ranked among the best in the country by Consumers Reports) for $6. Then go to McConnell's and for about $1 more, buy a pint of their vanilla. Next have a friend prepare a blind test for you. The difference will be enormous.... From now on your ice cream experience will be tremendous... Enjoy!

Categories: Wine Bars, Restaurants, Indian, Australian, Bars, American (New), Nightlife | Review: The food was mediocre at best. Came with a large party and had called for a reservation. We were not told that the restaurant had a policy to not serve a la carte to a bigger party and that the price would be $50 per person present. When we got there, this was essentially forced upon us without any prior knowledge. Much too expensive for the quality of food received.
Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Much better in person but solid Thai food when you are craving.

Categories: Sandwiches, Vegan, Cafes, Vegetarian, Restaurants | Review: First things first--who vomited the decor in this dirty pig-sty that looks like it should be featured on Restaurant Impossible? The only thing is, this place does good business--which bewilders the humble dining palates who joined us in SB. The food is nothing more than Meh!!! To name the two dishes I remember (party of six--two from LA, and two from SF and two from SB) Ginger Tofu Wonton Pillows (a 2.5 star. The garlic is way overpowering and some barely cooked ginger pieces made it at best 2.5 out of 5 stars). Two of us sampled the Tempeh Reuben as well. It was a 1-star at the very most!  It was so bland and dry--cutting corners by skimping on the sauerkraut and not worth the $10.50--served with a bland pasta bathed in veganaise. This place--I cannot even believe it can keep its doors open considering its filthy bad-decor atmosphere and inflated prices serving food that is less than mediocre. So unbelievable considering the reviews. None of the party of my "humble" six thought their dinners were good. All of them said "meh!" I have eaten at food trucks that knock the socks off of this place (for half the price all over the country)! 

Wait staff just existing--not really in to it or passionate about their place (and obviously not their food--which TJ's has them beat in the frozen food isle). 

One customer (unclear if she worked there because many of the wait staff knew her) was at the bar with her chihuahua on her lap while the owner and other wait staff/management either turned a blind eye or petted the beast. I love dogs, but not in a restaurant. Can you say a serious health code violation--wash your hands staff!!! It reflects your dirty, unsanitary place of business with dirty windows and dessert cases...we couldn't even go there considering that they win the popularity contest for "best desserts" by the SB independent based of nothing more than local civilian votes. Clearly lack of fierce competition in this town to make much needed improvements in a place that resists change in fear that it might give you a run for your $money$!

If you own or manage the Sojourner, I hope you will take this review as true constructive criticism. You need to know that it is time to invest in getting a professional to do interior design and decor and you need a menu overhaul in a big way. For the time you have been in business not even having beer on tap??? Another example of just being cheap and cutting corners. This review comes from six patrons who all collaborated on this review. We all agree it is harsh, but oh so needed. We wish you all the best and will return only if there will be a sincere attempt to improve in all arenas aforementioned. 

Will go out again tomorrow, we all need a "do-over!"

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: Seems like this is the spot for the college kids. We were here for a bachelorette shindig, and this was the first stop on the party bus. We felt a bit out of place, but oh well. We drank our 1 drink and moved on. The location is great, right in the middle of everything, with open air doors and easy to get in and out. 

The drinks took FOREVER. I mean at least 20 minutes to get through the line and get our drinks. They were a bit on the spendy side but they did come with a packet of Pop-Rocks, which was random yet kinda cool. We all reminisced about our childhood as we had parties in our mouths (DON'T!! Stop yourself, perv!). 

They play good music, but since we were maybe the only mid to late 30's crowd there, we felt sort of out of place. I would recommend if you're a younger crowd looking for the happening spot, then this may be your place.

Categories: Food, Hawaiian, Restaurants, Gluten-Free, Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, American (New) | Review: Yay finally a creative idea for a new yummy restaurant in IV that is tropical and sweet

Categories: French, Food, Restaurants | Review: Great service and the food amazing! The shrimp skewers were out of this world and an authentic French waiter. We walked up without a reservation and were seated outside on the lively patio. Reminded us of the Champs de élysées!

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Desserts, Wine Bars, Bars, Italian, Nightlife | Review: Chef Mollie's celebrated name (and client list) intrigued me as a recent transplant from Miami to Santa Barbara. There is no shortage of fine Italian and Mediterranean food in Miami, and I hoped that Trattoria Mollie might be Montecito's answer to the culinary hole left in my heart by South Florida's sumptuous Il Gabbiano and Prime Italian. Without doing much research, I booked a reservation for my family to experience Mollie. Despite our reservation, the hostess seemed annoyed by our arrival and curtly offered us a cramped table by the entrance. Knowing the gusts of cold air from the door and foot traffic would distract from what we hoped would be an exceptional evening, we requested a seat at one of the -MANY- empty tables in the back. She coolly told us all those tables were reserved (note: we stayed from 7:00 -9:40 and the tables never once saw a patron), but agreed to move our small party of three to an awkwardly large six-top one table over. What a compromise.  The table was set incorrectly, and the first 15-20 minutes of our evening lacked a consistent waiter/waitress. While we tried to determine who would actually be serving us for the evening, we expressed our concerns to a nearby server, who told us that she was sorry, but had "no control" over the logistics of who served us or where we sat, but that as long as the food was good, service "didn't matter." I deeply disagree with that philosophy when dining at a restaurant that charges between $30-$90 for a plate of pasta; but service did seem to pick up slightly after we ordered our meals, which included soft Rusack wine, a farm-fresh prosciutto and melon appetizer, three of the evening's specials (two white truffle pasta dishes and a delicate lasagna), followed by an impossibly fluffy lemon cheesecake, decadent tiramisu and some après dinner cordials. The food was, indeed, delicious. Mollie created beautifully fresh flavors reminiscent of many of the meals I experienced in Italy, with a modern update on each that kept them from feeling tired or heavy. She uses a subtle blend of exquisite ingredients that speak for themselves, letting the pasta and accoutrements become secondary to the nuanced flavor pairings championing the evening. She was visible in the kitchen and prep area, a lovely testament to the care and passion she obviously has for her craft. Indeed, the food was great - as expected and on par with other restaurants of its caliber. That said, the service really detracted from an otherwise superlative experience in a way that cheapened the meal and made it seem overly inflated and insincere. I love Italian food because it's often a celebration of the easy camaraderie between the kitchen and diners themselves, but this experience was cold and stilted. Our server offered little insight into the menu other than pushing the special dish du jour hard - which happened to be the most expensive item on the menu (a fact which we only discovered after our bill came) - and generally treated us like we should be so lucky to be dining chez Mollie, rather than a guest or customer.  If this is the norm for service, I wouldn't recommend or return to Trattoria Mollie, sadly opting for lesser quality cuisine at an establishment that valued my patronage and seemed to genuinely enjoy the dining experience over a great Zagat review.

Categories: Food, Restaurants, Desserts, Wine Bars, Bars, Italian, Nightlife | Review: Wow!!! Is that how Mollie treats president Obama and Oprah? I have not gone and tried the food yet but why would I go and try it when Mollie is really rude to her customers on the phone. I called the other night to confirmed my reservation when Mollie answered and I mention Mollie that I made a reservation for Thursday and Mollie just hung up on me, not saying anything. I called back and Mollie answered again and I told her that I'm not sure if she just accidentally hung up on me. She answer back really rude and told me, "what do I want?".   I would not want to go too a place when owner/chef talks to her customers like that. I will definitely not recommend this place to people.

Categories: Italian, Restaurants | Review: Trattoria was awesome... first tijme eating Italian in SB, and I have to say i'm impressed. The server was very professional and personal, food was excellent. He listed the night's specials, and recommended the  ravoli with stuffed prime rib when asked about it. Only thing is I wished there was more than 6-8 pieces of ravoli. But working in the food industry, I understand using premium cuts such as prime rib is costly, and would look unattractive raising the prices on the menu to cover the cost. The server was kind enough however to bring me extra bread without asking so that I could dip it with the sauce & also recommended me a good syrah to pair with my meal. Overall great experience, food, service, and atmosphere.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Lounges, Seafood, Spanish, Italian, Nightlife, Mediterranean | Review: Had a great experience here! The ambiance and the food was excellent. The service was on par. Our waiter, Justin, was very delightful and charming. He made us feel very welcomed during our first time here. If you are looking for a restaurant in Santa Barbara that has amazing food and a beautiful setting, this is the place! I will definetly come back on my next trip to Santa Barbara!

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: An average place for Thai food having lived in Los Angeles and East bay I know my expectation is high. This restaurant is in downtown prime location wiling dishes priced average $11. The portion could have been bit more. Ordered chicken pad see ew and green curry. The pad see ew was good but the green curry was bland and too watery. Bottom line won't be visiting this place again for thai food as there is too many other cuisine options in Santa Barbara. I will stay with my east bay restaurants for Thai food
Categories: Bakeries, Coffee & Tea, Donuts, Food | Review: Horrible customer service very rude and no iced black TEA when every other state in the country I have visited has it

Categories: Sandwiches, Vegan, Cafes, Vegetarian, Restaurants | Review: THe food is not  bad, but not extraordinarily tasty or unique in any way. My friend told me it's organic food (I'm not sure though). It's still a good place to hang out with friends, but not the best choice if you want to impress your tastebud.

Categories: Cajun/Creole, Breakfast & Brunch, Caterers, Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, Burgers | Review: Had our last meal (brunch) here before we left Santa Barbara. 
For a party of four, we feasted!

This is what we ordered:

Breakfast Jambalaya with eggs
French toast - comes with powdered sugar on top (didn't get any toppings though)
Crab cake Benedict
