Side of Apple honey sausage 
Beignet - covered with powdered sugar
Crawfish etouff 

Bloody mary with soju!
Mimosa

Everything was pretty good. Large portions!
Love that the meals is a fusion between american-breakfast + southern-creole!
Categories: Thai, Vegetarian, Noodles, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: The food was mediocre, the service was awful, and they couldn't figure out how to split a check. I will not be returning.

Categories: Garage Door Services, Restaurants, Live/Raw Food, Food, Home Services, Mediterranean, Keys & Locksmiths, Burgers, Vegetarian, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Vegan, Salad | Review: AN ABSOLUTE MUST! I am so beyond grateful for such delicious high-quality food like this!! As a vegan, I hope anybody looking for a special dining experience will try these amazing dishes! EVERY component is full of flavor and you can tell it's well thought out.  The presentation is gorgeous and the flavors are magical. We had the empenadas and BLTA from their weekend brunch menu. Simply put,  I didn't want the meal to end.  But then came the guava cheesecake with a little baklava from the owner ;) SO creamy and not overly sweet, it had cashew almond whip too! Ah! sooooo yummy, just go if you can.  I'm positive sure anything from their menu is mind blowing.

Categories: Gelato, Food, Specialty Food, Desserts, Chocolatiers & Shops | Review: This is a great little chocolate shop right off of State Street in Santa Barbara. They have some really unique flavors, like cardamom, lotus, and lemon ginger. It's a little bit on the expensive side (for $7, you get a 3 piece gift box with three chocolates of your choice) but that's pretty comparable to other chocolate shops, especially once you consider the location and quality. 

The staff was very friendly and helpful, even making suggestions on flavors and giving us tips on how to best save money (for example, you can get your chosen chocolates a la carte, in a gift bag instead of in a box, and it is marginally cheaper). 

Stop in here if you're wandering down State Street and interested in some delicious chocolate, or need a gift for someone.

Categories: Restaurants, Middle Eastern, Bars, Nightlife, Mediterranean, Lebanese | Review: We waited for 45 minutes when we were told a 20 minute wait and about 10 groups went before us and we had arrived before them. Very disappointed the special drinks we horrendous. The only good thing was the lentil soup. Staff was not very pleasant. Waited in bar area very warm.

Categories: Asian Fusion, Seafood, Restaurants, Poke, Food | Review: Hilfsbereit, lecker und obwohl eigentlich ich schon zu war hat sich das them darum bemüht, dass wir noch etwas gutes zum Essen bekamen... jederzeit wieder.

Categories: Thai, Restaurants, American (New), Pop-Up Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Probably the worst tom kha soup I have ever had, and I eat at a thai place almost every other day! The soup was so milky, almost tasted like you are drinking whole milk, no coconut flavor, no lemongrass or ginger flavor. The spicy flat noodles was good. The portions are small for the price. Nothing out of the ordinary.

Categories: Tex-Mex, Restaurants, Vegetarian, Sandwiches | Review: This is a great spot to check out when you are just not craving a greasy burger and fries.  I never thought that day would come for me but if you're interested in feeling better eg; not having grease dripping from your pores when you leave a restaurant, this place is a good bet.
    They have lots of different options for healthy eats and even if you have a friend that's not a health nut - there are lots of yummy things to order.  It's definitely worth checking out.

Categories: Pizza, Gluten-Free, Salad, Restaurants, Fast Food | Review: It's hard to rate Blaze because I've had a different experience (and different-tasting pizza) every time I've visited, even though I order the same thing each time... Given that it's inconsistent, I'd say my average experience would be rated 3 stars, although I've definitely had visits where I'd rate it 5 stars or even 1 star. 

I always order the Green Stripe, which consists of chicken, red peppers, garlic, and a pesto drizzle. Although the Green Stripe doesn't come with a base sauce, I always opt to add a base sauce of either red sauce, pesto, or both. 

*TIP: You can actually ask them for 2 base sauces, and can even get them mixed, which is yummy!*

However, what you get really depends on the day, or employees who take your order, as I've had times where the employees switched my order with someone else's, skimped out on toppings, slathered on way too much sauce (or not enough), messed up my order, overbaked/underbaked the pizza or were out of ingredients. My worst experience was definitely when they messed up my order twice in one visit, leading me to wait over 30 minutes for a pizza that was supposed to be "Fast Fire'd." Although they remade my pizza after the first mishap, they didn't add the sauce I wanted and brushed me off when I pointed this out to them. 

I would say that when the pizzas turn out right, they are pretty good (they can be super oily though) and reasonably priced at just under $10. I know I've been here several times and will probably continue to frequent Blaze, but I've definitely become more wary and go in with lower expectations (which is honestly a pretty standard practice here in IV). My best advice is to just be aware of what's going on with your pizza, and speak up if something goes wrong!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Bakeries, Desserts, Food Delivery Services, Patisserie/Cake Shop | Review: If you have a big sweet tooth I highly recommend this place. Unfortunately I am not a big sweet tooth. There cookies are good and their ice cream is just as good but for 1 cookie probably was made with a whole pound of sugar. It was good but not for me. Maybe others may enjoy they were good and soft cookies.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Farmers Market, Tapas Bars, Food, Desserts, Nightlife, Asian Fusion, Restaurants, Tapas/Small Plates, Bars, Indonesian | Review: Wow! Wonderful service and best of all, amazing food. This is the perfect place to tickle your taste buds. We had the gado gado salad, Brussels sprouts, chicken bao and the Branzino dish tonight. That branzino dish keeps me coming time after time. It is an incredible Indonesian dish!  The curry at the bottom of the fish is out of this world. To be  honest I have been dreaming about this for the last week and my only mission tonight was to come for this. The fish is cooked to perfection, it will not disappoint ! The spicy curry is like no other. Drinks are delicious and unique too and the dessert is memorable. We had the banana fritters, a sweet center ripe banana fried with sweet flour and topped with chocolate syrup. So fresh! They're like little donuts. So delicious! What a way to end the night.

The only negative thing I have to say is that they automatically add 20% tip to your bill no matter your group size (only two of us). I hate that I don't get to decide how good my service was. It was kind of written into their menu so by the time you read it you're already sitting down and it's too late. When did this become the norm :(

Categories: Bubble Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food | Review: I'm a huge fan of this place. This is the first time I've seen so much selection and options: slushees, smoothies, protein shakes, tea, and milk tea ... all with boba! It's utterly delightful and the prices are great. I like the GI Jane protein shake with vanilla protein powder and extra boba. What can I say .... I like to chew my drinks :)

Categories: Food, Acai Bowls, Bakeries, Coffee Roasteries, Desserts, Coffee & Tea | Review: Lucky Llama is the best place I know of for Acaí bowls, if I have the time to drive from Santa Clarita it's always worth it just for the sake of acaí. 
Iced lattes are on point and their outdoor seating makes for a great place to sit and read.
If that wasn't enough they are just a short walk from the beach.

Categories: Caterers, Food, Event Planning & Services, Personal Chefs | Review: Chef Danny cooked for our dinner party and the food was excellent. Both he and his assistant were personable and a pleasure to have throughout the evening, they went above and beyond for our guests.  Would absolutely hire again.

Categories: Restaurants, Korean, Asian Fusion | Review: Tried only one thing, but it was very disappointing. Had the kimchi jjigae, and it was bland, watered down, and intended to be unoffensive rather than being authentic. Choi's Oriental Market has much better Korean food in SB.

Service was good though.

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: Picked this place based on the 4 star yelp review.  It's a solid 4 star.  The tapas are variation on traditional Spanish tapas.  Drinks well made and good wine selection.

Categories: Breweries, Nightlife, Food, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Restaurants, Brewpubs, Gastropubs | Review: This was not a good experience. We walked in and asked for a menu as we hadn't eaten there before. The hostess was wearing a shirt open with her bra showing. Like buttoned down to show her bra. My husband and sons were with me and we were all uncomfortable. We turned around and left.

Categories: Restaurants, Pizza | Review: After sampling dozens of fantastic pizzerias in Chicago, New York, and Boston I know I'm not supposed to really like this place.  But somehow, I do.  

Don't get me wrong, its not the second coming of Giorodano's or Patsy's or anything, but its good stuff and makes for a great beach pizza

Categories: Vietnamese, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, Wine Bars, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Noodles | Review: Horrible food; it's the worst Vietnamese food I've ever eaten in my entire life. BE WARNED! These Vietnamese scoundrels are taking advantage of the customer's ignorance as to what the food suppose to taste like, not like a bunch of fried **** they can pull out of their *** in seconds. And in addition, to make heavy profit out of them! If I could give this restaurant a negative infinity score, I would.
Categories: Delis, Sandwiches, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Best and freshest sandwiches and salads ever! I wish I was in Santa Barbara more often.

Categories: Breweries, Nightlife, Food, Cocktail Bars, Bars, Restaurants, Brewpubs, Gastropubs | Review: Drinks we're good but service was terrible. Our waitress never checked on us. I've been here multiple times but service did not consistent. It depends on who is your waiter.

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Bars, American (New), Cocktail Bars | Review: "I don't want no scrub. A scrub is a guy that can't get no love from me
Hanging out the passenger side
Of his best friend's ride
Trying to holler at me"

These great words where written in 1999, by the famous R&B group TLC. This was the theme of my mentality as I strolled into the Honor Bar. I wanted to see if this place was as amazing as it sounded. Backed by the Houston group I was expecting some "honorably" things. Here's the low down on my experience.

First impression: they are everything, and honor bar is beautiful. Very small. Very comfy. And very stylish. I recognized some of Montecito's  finest dining as I walked in the door. Oprah was in the back, and my homie jack Johnson was sipping a scrimshaw at the bar. It seemed like the "happening" was happening at the honor bar.

Service: Lauren was our server, she did a great job. She was attentive and knew the answer to all of our question. She was also very knowledgable about the beverage menu, steering me towards a delicious Dragonette rose. Which btw, Rose is the next big thing. It's gonna blow up, people are gonna love Rose like they love getting laid and like they love eating ice cream. 

Food: forget the entrees I wanna talk about the aoili that came with the fries. I've never had anything like it. I believe it was chili flakes and sesame seed oil mixed with mayo, so good. 

Drank: rose and a Paloma. There's nothing more to be said.

Overall the place was bomb and I highly recommend it. 

10/10 would Rose again!

Categories: Sandwiches, Delis, Pizza, Italian, Restaurants | Review: Craving italian food? Please check out Basil's! Authentic and delicious are my favorite words to describe this special spot. Family owned and run, great food, you can't go wrong!
Categories: Specialty Food, Chocolatiers & Shops, Food, Desserts | Review: Chef Jean Michel is charming and talented.. Highly recommended.. Stop by, you won't be disappointed

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: A great place to come for a night out.  Very stylish interior, yummy drinks, friendly staff, and tasty food.  Nice rack is my fav!  Their drinks are always great and creative.  They had lavender and honey martinis as a special drink one night and it was soooo good.  Definitely worth a try!
Categories: Food, Bakeries | Review: The bread is wonderful and the people who work there are so friendly and helpful! This bakery is definitely going places!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Seafood | Review: This is officially my new favorite Mexican restaurant in town!  

Staff, food, music, price.... everything was lovely!

Categories: Pizza, Food, Restaurants, Bubble Tea, Sandwiches, Food Delivery Services | Review: Finally, pizza with real flavor! We'd been searching this area for good pizza for so long that we'd all but given up. When we heard about Pizza Online we thought we'd cross our fingers and give it a try. I'm happy to report that we've found our place!

Ordering online was super easy. We chose an extra large (16") that comes with a 'regular crust' with pepperoni and mushrooms. The crust was substantial while somehow managing to be light and crisp at the same time. We chose mozzarella cheese which was plentiful, as were the toppings. The best part of all was the sauce - thick and flavorful with traditional pizza sauce seasonings. (not sweet like Rusty's)

On the business side of things, I can say that the price was excellent. With tax and (small) tip, our total came to about $13. Can't beat that! As an added convenience, the website allows you to set a pickup time. You can place your order and pick it up in 15 minutes, or choose a specific time. On football Sunday we estimated when halftime would take place and chose our pickup time accordingly. It was ready when we arrived, still steaming hot and delicious. We were greeted warmly and offered red peppers and parmesan cheese. The gentleman asked us how the online ordering went and then told us that he was the one who created the website! Big thumbs up on all accounts!

Pizza Online Company is a great locally-owned business that should be supported. I encourage everyone to give it a try!

Categories: American (New), Wraps, Chinese, Sushi Bars, Barbeque, Dim Sum, Asian Fusion, Ramen, Restaurants, Noodles, Soup, Japanese, Hot Pot | Review: I tried Asie for the first time todayamazing food and wonderful owners. My new, go to place in Santa Barbara!

Categories: Restaurants, Fish & Chips, Food, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, American (Traditional), Seafood | Review: The restaurant is very clean and a nice atmosphere. Must order the Ahi burger it is amazing. Their chipotle sauce is also one of a kind.

Categories: Comfort Food, Asian Fusion, Chinese, Tapas/Small Plates, Noodles, Thai, Restaurants | Review: Hands down one of my favorite Thai restaurants. I love the simple menu. You can't go wrong with any of their noodle dishes. My favorite is the Bangkok Street noodle and Boat noodle. Perfect during a rainy day.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional) | Review: Stopped off on my way back to the Cruz as I had a craving for corn beef hash and eggs. Here's my thoughts on this Korean mom & pop run American eatery which I give a generous 2.5 stars.

Service 3-4 stars
Woman who acts like the owner greets you with a warm smile and big hello. Gent who acts like her husby cleared and cleaned the table. She brought piping hot coffee and took my order graciously. I dislike having to ask for refills as I did here. But can be expected when lack of restaurant experience shows especially when table occupancy is 1/3rd full.

Food 2 stars
I asked for well done, crispy corn beef, wheat toast, eggs over medium. The CBH barely had a crust, the toast brought out 10 minutes b4 the eggs and CBH, so by the time I got the real food they were cold. Not only that they barely were toasted. Commercial packaged jam on the table too. Hmmm. Lets not forget the canned CBH for $10.95. Wow, the import tax for Carpenteria on canned goods must be #1 in the nation. Is it me or am I spoiled? I'm used to mom & pop small eateries serving homemade jams and free hot muffins and decent coffee, not Denny's style packaged drip coffee, and homemade CBH. Got it, Hugos doesn't.

Will I go back? Nope. For what I was charged I'll head off to Cajun Kitchen for a real breakfast. Don't get me wrong, everyone is friendly here it's just w-a-y overpriced for standard breakfast fare. 

Hey, maybe I'll return if you serve up Korean breakfast items like rice & eggs with some banchchan served with kalbee, or bulgogi, or dak galbi, and so on.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Found this restaurant on Yelp and I'm so glad I stopped by on my through Santa Barbara. I ordered the Agaves Enchiladas ( halibut and shrimp) and they were spectacular. The enchilada sauce was a little spicy but so good and different than any other enchiladas. The side salad of dark green lettuce and fruit with their dressing was a good balance with the enchiladas!

Categories: Nightlife, Restaurants, Steakhouses, Bars | Review: We came here after a long day of softball after hearing  how amazing this place was. We tried to call hours before and make a reservation. No one answered the phone. We wait around at the door and finally someone helps out. The wait is about an hour so we wait. The hostess was nice.  We sit down and the place is super dark. The service was pretty good. Not personal but good. It took really long to get our food and one of ladies with us didn't get her food until after we ate. I guess the kitchen messes up. When her husband asked to have it comped because everyone was finished eating. They just took off the soup. That was odd. Everything was just blah. Nothing special.  Really big portions though and huge prime rib. I didn't love my meal
Or experience. I wouldn't go back. Just not really my taste  or great experience.  I don't know what all the rave is about this place. It's just blah. It also has really old decor inside.  OH AND THEY RAN OUT OF RIB EYE. ONLY 3 plates left. Isn't this a steak house ???

Categories: Delis, Gluten-Free, Fast Food, Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: Not only were they out of just about everything You could think of, we waited an hour to get our food, and my order still came out wrong. Sandwich was mid
Categories: Dance Clubs, Nightlife | Review: Bomb DJ, no cover, fun bartenders, great drinks, oh and they have beer pong! Could a girl ask for more?!

Categories: Restaurants, Fast Food, Burgers | Review: Got to say, I'm a fan.  The In-N-Out name here isn't exactly accurate, as you always end up waiting for your food here--a mix of them making it fresh, and the long line of cars and people who also want said food.  This location also has a habit of closing off one of the car entrances for no apparent reason, but you can maneuver around it anyway.  Service is good, the place is clean, blah blah blah; y'all know this place, so I won't drone on or tell you there's a secret menu like that's suddenly a surprise or something.

I always get a double here.  I've had them hovering between medium and medium well, but never well done which is good.  Beef is good quality and otherwise it's a well made fast food cheeseburger.  The fries are nice and fresh but unremarkable otherwise; get them Animal style (melted cheese, grilled onions, thousand island dressing) to kick them up a notch, or just use salt and ketchup.  I've only had a vanilla shake but they are extra thick and tasty as well.  In-N-Out is a cult classic for a reason: the food is consistently great for what it is.

Categories: Restaurants, Bars, Irish, Nightlife, Pubs, Gastropubs | Review: Food is just OK here but then again, it's not really known as a restaurant, it's best for the beer selection and warm feeling. 

It's great for meeting friends and enjoying a pint but the bar food is less to be desired.

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese | Review: This place reminds me of one of my favorite spots in Chinatown in SF. If you want authentic chinese foodin goleta, this is the spot to go to! I probably come here at least once every other week. Once you order, you get a salad AND soup. I usually get their salt and pepper spare ribs, generals chicken, mongolian beef or all of the above and take home the leftovers. Some say the owner doesn't give out hugs anymore, this is not the case for me as she ALWAYS gives me that hug before I leave (maybe because i've been coming here for so long) Do yourself a favor and get the dinner portion of whatever you choose, you'll thank me later for it:)

Categories: Breweries, Restaurants, Sports Bars, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Food, Bars | Review: Super tasty food and (usually) friendly staff. One time the host took us to the back part to eat which was super cool and I didn't even know existed! Only downside I had was twice asked for no bacon on the chicken sandwiches I got and it came out with bacon (I just took it off so as not to be a pain). The food is usually pretty tasty and they have some great appetizers. Their own beers are decent as well. Overall, recommended.

Categories: Vegan, Vegetarian, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: I'm not really understanding the hype about this place.  The sandwiches are average and the prices are too high.

I went to the location in Isla Vista once before and then came to this location last week.  Both times I had the same feeling: the sandwiches aren't bad, but they're far from great.  The sauces they use have a vaguely artificial, MSG-like flavor.  They taste good for a few bites but by the end of the sandwich they've worn out their welcome.  The overall quality is on the level of Subway (which, ironically, used to occupy this location) or similar fast-food sandwich places.

The biggest problem for me is the price.  The sandwiches here cost $10-12 each.  That is not exorbitantly expensive in itself, but given the fast-food-level product I don't really understand how they think they can get away with it.  There are several places in town (Metropulos and Three Pickles come to mind) where you can get a sandwich for the same or lower price that is far better --- not just a little better, but leagues and eons better, so much better that it leaves Ike's in the dust.  You can go to the Lucky Penny in the midst of the hip and bustling Funk Zone and get a sandwich for maybe a dollar more.  Heck, even right across the street is Garrett's, where you can get a sit-down meal for what they're charging for a sandwich at Ike's.  I just can't see any reason to choose an Ike's sandwich when there are so many better options available.

This location also seems to still have a few kinks to work out logistically.  The only menus are taped onto the glass at the counter and cram so many sandwich options onto a small piece of paper that the writing is tiny.  They said they plan to add a menu board above the kitchen (and you can see the space where it should go), but it's not there yet.  They don't seem to have figured out which way the line should go, so two lines formed in opposite directions from people who came in the front and back doors.  Hopefully they'll work these issues out.

I wouldn't call Ike's bad, but that's about all I can say.  If I were driving through some little town and came across Ike's I might grab a sandwich.  But given that I live here and know the alternatives, I can't really see myself coming here again.  Whenever I go to a place like this I can only hope that they take the time to visit some other sandwich places in town and see what they're up against.  To my mind, trying to offer this level of sandwich at these prices is, frankly, ludicrous.  Ike's is heavily outclassed by many other local sandwich shops.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Questo posto merita veramente!
Le migliaia di recensioni avevano perfettamente ragione nell'affermare che il cibo era ottimo.
I burrito sono grossi ma non enormi, ripieni di ottimo pollo, carne o pesce e accompagnati da riso e insalata.
La Guacamole è fresca e abbondante e le chips si lasciano mangiare senza opporre resistenza.
Occhio perché prima di sedersi bisogna ordinare (e pagare) alla cassa per poi attendere al tavolo con il proprio numerino.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Everything was under or over seasoned. 

The best dishes were the appetizers (mussels, chowder, oysters)... The other food was forgettable and what you would expect from a harbor restaurant. 

The view is gorgeous tho. The upstairs is the full restaurant. There was a wait but their buzzer allowed us to walk around the marina until our name was called. I would stick to the fresher seafood and I think you'll be ok.

Categories: Health Markets, Coffee & Tea, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Restaurants, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Breakfast & Brunch, Food, American (New), Specialty Food | Review: These are the best acai bowls maybe on earth. Where does acai come from? Maybe it's better there, but you probably can't get them as beautiful. And you can get anything off this menu (even the spinach one!) and it is reliably fantastic. No stray seeds (a smooth blend), no weird chalky flavor (tempered properly with honey), and fresh fruit. I wish they didn't close early but I get it.

Plus they do stamp cards!

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: Ice cream was of high quality and very tasty. We got the Churro Con Leche and Brazillian Coffee Chip and both were outstanding. No line at 6pm on Friday night. 

+5 for outstanding ice cream. Priced at $4.25 for a single which was a bit on the high side but worth it for the quality.

A small/single is enough for a sharing couple. Just ask for half scoop of 2 flavors and you got yourself a couple's treat.

-1 for price and customer service that was lackluster.

Categories: Delis, Gluten-Free, Fast Food, Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: Great sandwich shop! Super friendly service and the sandwiches were great. I highly recommend the chicken parm. The pickles are also delicious!

Categories: Arts & Entertainment, Food, Bars, Nightlife, Wine Bars, Wineries, Wine Tasting Room | Review: About the only thing I love more than wine tasting is vintage Sci-Fi, so when my friend and I were out tasting in the Funk Zone this past Saturday and I saw the name of this place, I just knew we had to stop in!

Area 51 is the common name of a highly classified United States Air Force facility located within the Nevada Test and Training Range.  Fans of Stargate: SG-1 (moi!) can tell you that all the coolest and most high-tech alien Gizmos have been stored there by the US Government, and if you want to hang out with Little Green Men it's unquestionably the place to go ;-)

This little gem of a tasting room has a similar name, and shamelessly (in a most interesting and intriguing fashion) riffs on the Area 51 concepts and names in an attempt to make Area 5.1 stand out a bit from the dozens of other tasting rooms in SB's Funk Zone.  They're efforts are highly successful - if you take the time to pay this place a visit I'm confident you'll find it most enjoyable.

Tip: Their tasting flight consists of five pours of wines with Alien themed names like "Close Encounter", "White Light', and "Zinvasion".  The cost of a tasting is $20.

Santa Barbara (and the rest of California) seems to be about to do away with most of the Pandemic restrictions; Area 5.1 was offering both indoor and outdoor tasting when we were there.

While the wines aren't in the same category as those you'll find at Melville or Riverbench, we found all the wines on the tasting list very approachable and reasonably priced.  My favorite pour of the day was unquestionably the 2019 "Conspiracy Red" blend.  It's both spicy and sweet, with hints of vanilla, delicate notes of dark berries, and very mild tannins.  The bottle price is $36; it's the highest priced (and besting tasting) of the five wines on the tasting list.

We very much enjoyed the tasting experience at Area 5.1 - I look forward to returning and trying their new offerings in the future!
Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Coffee Roasteries | Review: Love this spot! Great customer service,Great coffee and their pastries are so so yummy!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Nightlife, Bars | Review: Stopped here for lunch on our way home from NorCal.  We wanted to go to a Seafood Restaurant, but the parking was bad & it's a walk to the boardwalk.  We were under time schedule, so we were happy to see El Torito, thinking it would be a good buffet brunch.  But this one was really terrible - except for our waiter, Justin.  he was good.  The food looked left over & was just being warmed.  The Omellete maker was brand new & was practicing.  The whole experience was bad.  It sure gave a bad rap for the name El Torito, the way we've experienced El Torito.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: Best Italian food in SB!  Super expensive, but worth the splurge for a good meal.  Make reservations.  I don't know what the corking fee is, but I do remember the wine list being SUPER expensive, so you might want to look into that.   The squid ink pasta was amazing and fun, lamb was good, appetizers were good

Categories: Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, American (Traditional), American (New), Southern | Review: We stumbled on this jem on our daytrip to Solvang.  First of all it is lovely.  A beautiful decor that is very reminiscent of the old south with the brick wall.  Then oh my God the food was fantastic.  I ordered the Egg, Spinach and Bacon scrambled with buttermilk biscuit.  It was divine!  Lord Have Mercy!  is all I can say.  Now I'm from New Orleans so this hearty breakfast was the BEST I have ever had!  My husband ordered beignets and that was a disappointment because we would call those hush puppies down home but it still was tasty.  Very nice alll around and the wait staff was professional and available.  
We'll definitely stop by there again when we are up north.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (New), Food | Review: It was good, good service, fast, you can make it healthy and it tastes pretty good but honestly nothing memorable or special. I probably wouldn't go back..

Categories: Szechuan, Chinese, Nightlife, Karaoke, Asian Fusion, Restaurants | Review: In two words, NOT GOOD! Ok they want me to elaborate about the food at this mediocre Chinese whatever the hell their serving is just not good! 
Actually it's pretty bad. We tried four dishes and all it was, is soy sauce on top of soy sauce on each and every dish. Terrible, no one in on a Friday night, no smell of anything when you walk in? weird & not very inviting! I tried to make this short but yelp wanted more dirt to post. We live close by and really wanted this to be good, it isn't...................save your money, Trader Joes line, Trader Ming is much better!!!!!!!!

Categories: Thai, Restaurants | Review: Simply delish.  Between the four of us we split the Chicken Satay, Chicken Pad See Ew, Panang Curry, and some spicy mint shrimp dish.  All of the dishes I had were some of the best Thai dishes I've tried.  Everything tasted fresh, and the meat was never scary, fatty, or overcooked.  The service was fine, no complaints there.  We went on a Saturday night during their peak time and only had to wait about 15 minutes.  The atmosphere was nice with intimate tables.  My mom said she went on a separate occasion and claimed the won ton soup was the best she'd ever had because of its generous portion and robust flavor.

Categories: Nightlife, Bars, Sports Bars | Review: Wandered in here one Friday after work when I had to wait an for my ride home.  Place is clean, bartender attentive and drinks cheap.  Good, chill place to sit back and kill some time.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: Thanks to Tony and the team at Bouchon for a wonderful meal.  Tony helped select some really memorable wines for our group and the food was top to down amazing.  The duck was possibly the best I've ever had.

Categories: Restaurants, Thai | Review: I am a huge fan of Pataya! This is the first restaurant to really spark my interest in Thai food. I was a regular here for about two years and I have never had a problem with the food or service,. The owners are incredibly sweet and accommodating, and the food is consistently phenomenal! I highly recommend the Pa Nang Chicken!

Categories: Lounges, Bars, American (New), Nightlife, Modern European, Restaurants | Review: I absolutely ADORE Cafe Luck.  The food, the service, the atmosphere...everything is always stellar.  I see that a few reviewers have had poor experiences with the staff, but this has NEVER been the case for me.  My boyfriend and I, recent graduates, come here often.  Service is always attentive and kind, despite the fact that we are young (which sometimes makes a difference).  

Moving onto the food...I LOVE it.  On our most recent visit, we had the pleasure of sampling the specialty appetizer of the day: pork belly sliders.  It was divine...pork belly braised in duck fat, topped with a quail egg, frisee and heirloom tomato.  I exclaimed to my boyfriend "This is what heaven tastes like!"  

We also order our regular favorites, the Cafe Luck Burger, medium.  It was great as always, but sometimes I find the bacon to be a bit overpowering. 
We also always get the Mac n Cheese with truffled ham.  Let me tell you, this is the BEST Mac n Cheese I have ever had.  I have tried a lot of Mac n Cheese, made several recipes, but when I crave Mac, this is the one.  It's so divine with its perfect bubbly crust and hint of truffle...I dream about this stuff. The Beef Stroganoff with wild mushroom is also excellent as well.  A rich but comforting dish.

Their cocktails are great too, and I love the little macaroons they give you with your check.  It's so sweet.  

Anyways, Cafe Luck is one of my go-to spots in Santa Barbara, when I am feeling like a casual romantic night out.  It's always charming and the food is delicious.  A definite favorite...heart.

Categories: Restaurants, Event Planning & Services, American (Traditional), Venues & Event Spaces | Review: Harry's has been around for decades and doesn't disappoint. I ordered the sirloin steak sandwich with the house made coleslaw. The steak dinner is prepared to perfection. The house made slaw is    slightly sweet but savory as well. The French fries are NOT frozen but cut here on site. The "Harry Mary" (bloody Mary) is made with Seagram's Vodka, a slice of swiss cheese and a small slice of salami..... Oh.....did I forget the green olive? Very nice drink. The booths are red leather with brass tacks. This place ooze's class. The meals are reasonably priced here. This is a good old fashioned american style restaurant with very loyal customers.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian | Review: Had dinner here last night. Slow Sunday evening- only 5 tables occupied.

Not impressed with the wine list at all- if you like quality chardonnay like Rombauer or Sonoma Cutrer- you are out of luck- I believe they only have two chardonnays by the glass- mine for $14 was absolutely horrible.

Soup of the day claimed to be puree of asparagus though was definitely creamed spinach. Lasagna special was overcooked and had peas in it?
The caprese salad was good- wish for more tomatoes but the basil was fresh and delicious. Duck ravioli was the best part of our meal. 

Staff not as attentive- everyone was in auto mode- no personal touch at all which is too bad. I believe you get what you pay for but $100 for two pasta dishes, unpleasant two glasses of wine and salad is bit too much. We definitely experienced much better food and service here in SB.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), Seafood, French | Review: 0 stars if possible.

Absolute worst owner, service and worst dining experience ever.  Period.  They are not only pretentious, but their food isn't even unique enough to merit the attitude.  While just "good" taste wise, they are doing nothing unique, New American Bistro is a dime a dozen, and their lack of even the minimal care and concern for their diners experience makes them a disgrace to the industry.  Their piss poor attitude (evident by the lack of apology and the chef/owner himself telling us off for relaying our negative experience) coupled with their inability to effectively serve 40 people seated by their own reservation schedule is an impressive failure, to say the least.  If you're not ready to play the big boys game, on a Saturday night, you should do a better seating schedule, learn what the word hospitality means, learn how to serve multiple people efficiently or don't throw your hat in the ring AT ALL.  Getting "slammed" when all your tables aren't full and your restaurant is small with a 3 person line and your server doesn't  stop by the table ONCE during the 40 minute ticket time for entrees is disgraceful and a poor excuse for such bad treatment (especially when the server, Pete or Paul?, can be seen conversing for 10 minutes at the table NEXT to you).  However, when mistakes happen, as they will, you don't even know how to apologize appropriately. A simple "my apologies for your experience" could have rectified the whole evening.  Somehow that is beneath them and the Chef/owner would rather say "thanks for the attitude, ladies". You sir picked your profession, you chose to go into the hospitality industry, stop being pretentious and behaving like those choices did the general public a favor and that we (who pay your $50 per person avg) owe you anything.  I've been in the restaurant industry for over 20 years, and bravo Chef, you win for having been the biggest disappointment in those entire 20 plus years.  The art of hospitality and the industry itself is lost in your establishment. There's one honor you do deserve. 


Response to "Chef" :

Oh my gosh, thank you "Chef".  You validated my negative review! You are the reason chefs get the egotistical stereotype.  You mentioning my call only makes it better. It's true. I'm glad you heard it.    Not once did you apologize. But I like how you mention it was 5 ladies, acting like we were attacking you and you couldn't get in a word edgewise.  It was me, restaurant business for over 20 years as previously stated, a surgical oncologist, an internal medicine doctor, a cardiologist, and my daughter.  Only I and my sister, the surgical oncologist, spoke to you in a very controlled and polite manner until you showed complete lack of care or concern for the way we were treated at your place of business. Obviously, you should have worked at a real restaurant to learn how to run one, before owning one.  You paid for the title of "Chef", vs the ones who earn it through years of hard work.  

You may indeed work hard.  But you do not do it to guarantee an exceptional overall experience of you could care less about our experience and write it off as simply as you have.

I have been in the business and have been married to a real Chef long enough to know how hard the restaurant industry is.  But there are many restaurants who pull it together, exquisite cuisine, presentation, service and overall experience that go without the recognition that they deserve.  Please don't act like you are on the same level as they are. You should be able to pull off an amazing experience for the prices and recognition you are demanding.  All you offer are excuses. 

Thank you for reaffirming my thoughts and showing the yelp community just how egotistical you are.  

Love,
Karen

Categories: Cafes, Burgers, American (Traditional), Tacos, Seafood, Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Such a cute spot with great food and drinks. Chips & salsa to-go is a HUGE serving size. Everything is reasonably priced, and the dine-in outdoor seating is incredible. 

Ordered ahi tacos & red snapper (fresh catch of the day) - both were GREAT. Highly recommend!

Categories: American (New), Restaurants, Nightlife, Seafood, Bars | Review: Place is dirty, floor scattered with food leftovers. Service is terrible and arrogant, as if they are doing me a favor for serving me. Everyone from the host to the guy who cleans up the tables seem like they don't care.
Prices ridiculously high, food and drinks mediocre. Only thing they got going for them is the location, guess that's how the survive.
My first and last visit here. Stay away!

Categories: Szechuan, Chinese, Restaurants | Review: Came here for the first time late on Christmas Eve 2011. Everything we ordered was delicious! When the boy and I go out for Chinese we always start with some kind of fried app and hot and sour soup. This place has our favorite cream cheese wantons and their seafood hot and sour soup is AH-MAZING (I'm literally salivating just thinking about it). A little on the expensive side, but a wonderful treat every few months. The interior reminds me of the Chinese restaurants of my youth, the only thing missing is the lazy susan in the center of the table. Hands down my favorite Chinese place in the area.

Categories: Restaurants, American (New), French | Review: I ate there today and had the Cobb salad. There were pieces of lettuce that were nearly rotten.  I told the waiter he said, oh sorry about that. 
They do have good soup and hamburgers.  They just aren't careful about quality lettuce.

Categories: Coffee Roasteries, Acai Bowls, Food, Coffee & Tea, Sandwiches, Bagels, Restaurants, Cafes | Review: I can say that absolutely without a doubt hands down this is my favorite coffee shop especially in Santa Barbara just the ambiance alone it's absolutely great but the quality of the drinks here are on another level I got a matcha drink there they call it the Bella Rose (matcha latte) And it is out of this world I also like that they do milk substitutions but just the attention to quality even with the design of the drink shows a lot of attention and care one thing I will say is that they do not make drinks to go they do not offer to go cups so definitely if you're going to go here make sure you give yourself some time to sit and enjoy your drink  it sucks that I don't live around here because I would be there all the time if you're in this area don't mess around you got to check this place out you will not be disappointed

Categories: Nightlife, Soul Food, Restaurants, Comfort Food, Local Flavor | Review: The scene at 1:50 am after a night of checking out downtown Santa Barbara's bar scene, cold and hungry!! It was a little bit hard to find this hole in the wall, but finally did. I ordered the Ultimate that came with BBQ chicken, tater tots, bacon bits, tomato and jalepeño. It was a hearty combination that satisfied. You can even top that with cheetos! 

A diamond in the rough, but for llate night grub, this hits the spot!

Categories: Restaurants, Soup, Salad, Thai | Review: This is the best Thai restaurant in Santa Barbara, no questions asked, mic drop, review over..

Just kidding, I can't leave you hanging like that! But quite seriously, I've been exploring Thai food options all over the county for years and Menu Fan has never been beat. The quality, quantity, flavoring, and meal variety blow other places out of the water. They have options for GF and Vegan eaters too. 

While I almost always order take out so I can stuff my face and not by judged, the in-house environment here is actually semi-romantic and the table service I experienced was above average. It was a little slower than most but if you're on a date or spending time with friends and family, it's not noticeable at all, and actually quite enjoyable when the wait staff are not asking you questions every 10 seconds.  

Any who, my method of madness is calling in an order for pick up and sharing it with a friend, my boyfriend, or splitting it into a couple meals for myself for the week. The portions are very big and I would recommend sharing. My favorite order is the Sizzling Platter with chicken and no peanut sauce. Though I've also enjoyed yellow and red curry dishes. 

The parking lot is very tiny so if you are dinning in house during a popular eating time be ready to explore other options either on the street or in the Lazy Acres lot. It's never been that hard for me to find a space as the Mesa is pretty secluded from the downtown madness. All in all I've never had a bad experience and I would highly recommend checking it out.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (New), Food | Review: We really enjoyed our breakfast at Dawn Patrol. I was in the mood for Huevos rancheros and they definitely hit the mark on this one. I ordered it with over easy eggs and the sauce and freshly made tortilla strips were delicious. We also ordered the special of the day which was chorizo hash and a vegetarian hash with sweet potatoes, spinach and tomatoes topped with over easy eggs. All were enjoyed and we would definitely recommend Dawn Patrol to those visiting the Santa Barbara area and locals alike.

Categories: Restaurants, Food Delivery Services, Nightlife, Cocktail Bars, Food, Tapas Bars, Bars | Review: Great food and drinks! Very large menu to chose from and you could close yours eyes and point at an item without being disappointed! Also they serve moscow mukes in the copper tins which are great! Old cartoons playing above the bar gives a light and fun atmosphere!

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Venues & Event Spaces, Peruvian, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Nightlife, Latin American, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Tapas/Small Plates, Mexican | Review: Overpriced and very average food. We had the chicken for $28. It was a very ordinary chicken and grilled vegetables. Not at all worth the price. Chicken was dry and overcooked. Vegetables were undercooked and hard to digest. We also ordered the shrimp and crab enchiladas. It was very average, nothing special. A good fish taco would have much tastier.

Categories: Food, Specialty Food, Grocery, Delis, Bakeries, Restaurants, Health Markets | Review: Crazy expensive meat and bad policies. I still love my cashiers that stayed from when it was Vons but hate that they laid off the developmentally challenged guys. Bring back the Manager's Specials in the meat department.

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, Nightlife, Bars, American (New), Cocktail Bars | Review: Going down one more star because I cannot BELIEVE they have the gall to try to charge you a $5 upcharge when you ask for them to leave all the gristle and scrap dark chicken meat OFF of your salad! No classy restaurant would EVER even THINK about putting dark meat chicken and gristle on a very expensive salad to begin with. Copied and pasted my Facebook review for the full story:

This could easily be one of my favorite restaurants... as this place has SO much potential, with a bold and truly creative and talented chef. BUT, the management is obviously clueless. No one wants to pay $20 for a lunch salad with chicken on it, when half of the chicken you get is SCRAP meat! **NO ONE** wants dark meat and gristle on their salad. For that pricey of a (rather small) salad for lunch, it should be 100% breast meat! Others have said the exact same thing on Yelp reviews, and management doesn't respond - doesn't care. And get this! When I asked the waitress if I can get all white meat on the salad, she said that would be a $5 upcharge! Are you kidding me?? It's not like I am asking for an extra order of chicken on the salad. I am asking for the same amount of chicken, but just requesting that my very expensive salad not have any scrap meat on it!!!! Just because you are always busy does not mean that you can become complacent, not listening or caring about your customers concerns. It WILL effect you eventually, as you lose your loyal customers one by one.

Categories: Restaurants, Italian, American (Traditional), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: It makes me sad to give this little breakfast nook 1 star but it certainly needs some TLC. I was in there this past Sunday 9/3/17 and it was a real disaster. The hostess was super slow and out there. She was moving at a very slow pace and quoting everybody varying over exaggerated wait times. We finally got a table and we must have waited 15 minutes to get waters!!!! It was at least 20 minutes before our waiter greeted us. He's definitely a nice older gentleman with longer hair but is extremely slow and just not efficient. He took our order and pretty much messed everything up. The food took forever and the whole experience was really unpleasant. Writing this review in hope that the owner pays some attention to the staff. I've always know this to be a very quick and efficient little cafe. I talked to my family and friends about it because I was so surprised and they all shared the same sentiment about this place. We have been coming here for over 20 years. I hope this restaurant gets the attention and love it deserves in future months.... otherwise I don't see it lasting.

Categories: Sushi Bars, Japanese, Restaurants | Review: Those who have dined with me at sushi bars know that I dislike sushi rolls intensely. Those who have not are about to learn.

At omakase meals in Japan, or at proper sushi bars here in the U.S., sushi rolls are served at the very end of a meal as a means of ensuring the customer is full, and as a "f*ck you" from an exasperated chef who has fed you like Shamu for 2 hours, unloading all of his premium A+ stuff on you, even as you broke several of the many unwritten sushi chopstick rules.

Perhaps I exaggerate a tad, but so does everyone on Yelp.

Bottom line is that sushi bars that specialize in rolls can't be trusted and are often presided over by lackluster hacks who do not possess the skill to make the precise cuts required of sashimi and nigiri. After all, it is much easier to wrap 3 different types of seafood with avocado, imitation crab, sesame seeds, "spicy sauce," Worcestershire sauce, orange Cheetos dust, and Frosted Flakes. (Throw some ketchup in it too, because our American tastes are subhuman.)

For this reason, Sakana is very much the opposite of what I typically look for in a sushi bar. It is here that traditional sushi like sashimi and nigiri take a backseat to expensive rolls that combine multiple types of fish. Western concoctions like "sriracha cream cheese", "truffle soy sauce", and "habanero pepper aioli" find themselves on nearly EVERY dish, either painted on the plate in squiggly lines or as a fluorescent goo ominously drenching the food like the slime from "You Can't Do That on Television."

Beyond its incomprehensible culture-bending culinary point-of-view, Sakana is a trendy Instagram-darling of a restaurant situated in one of the most posh communities in the country, unapologetically catering its menu and prices towards trust-funders and (occasionally) their truly rich benefactors. This place violates so many of my sensibilities that I should challenge its owner to a Hamilton-style duel. And YET, I gotta be honest, Sakana kinda almost works for me.

Their "Habanero Yellowtail Melt Press Box" was perhaps the richest sushi dish I've ever eaten. What Sakana calls a "press box" is essentially a roll shaped inside a box without a seaweed wrapper. Call it whatever you want, but a circumcised sushi roll is still a sushi roll. Spicy as it was, this roll featured a bunch of different seafood, and both a habanero sauce and a truffle soy sauce, but it was surprisingly pleasing - both in terms of taste and in terms of its dramatic presentation, which resembled the type of high food art I'd expect at a Joël Robuchon outpost; it was shiny and colorful and wild, like a row of drum majorettes. It was as luxurious and caloric of a sushi splurge as I could imagine, and I could only imagine having this kind of dish in two places: Vegas or here in Montecito. But let's also be clear -- despite its merits, I'd not call this sushi, as I couldn't really discern any seafood under all of the sauces.

The "Garlic Albacore Kawari Sashimi" was closer to sushi, boasting a plate with seared albacore topped with garlic chips, chives, roasted ginger, dijon crab, and truffle soy sauce. The sinfully rich dijon crab-infused truffle sauce stole the show. 

What else didn't work for me? The nigiri I ordered had rice that was too tightly-packed, and the uni was not great despite being in Santa Barbara. Better than I'd get in most places, but I expect it to be transcendent in this region. Most detrimentally, everything was super expensive.

Attempting to come to a conclusion about this place, I am faced with a dilemma: despite not conforming to my sushi preferences, the food here is quite good and the interaction with my chef at the sushi bar was friendly, informative, and professional - but there are just too many things about Sakana that turn me off for me to recommend it. For an area with other worthy Japanese dining alternatives (such as the outstanding Yoichi's in downtown Santa Barbara), I believe one must really LOVE rolls for this restaurant to be a priority.

Food-Décor-Service: 4.1/4.0/4.4

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars | Review: Hands down one of my favorite bars ever. I live about 2-3 hours away depending on traffic but every chance I get I want to make the drive! Great atmosphere, super friendly staff and incredible drinks! If coffee brings you even the slightest bit of happiness, DO NOT hesitate to order the Coffee Negroni. You will not be disappointed!

Categories: Seafood, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Had the most amazing lunch and drinks! Our bartender Sheryl was SUPERB! She was very friendly and made our experience there extra special! We can not wait to return!!! Love, Houston, Tx couple:)

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: First and foremost, I am pleased with the food, and the portions are very generous. 
*However
The general attitude of the staff makes me think twice about going in or not. Today was the third time that my "Hellos" have been downright ignored. The girls at the front of the store are very unwelcoming. Their poor dispositions and cold STARES make me feel as if I'm burdening them with my ordering food at all.
I'm a reasonable person, so I just thought I'd lay my honest opinion down

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Food, Desserts | Review: Very good (but not great) premium ice cream with a large variety of flavors including some standards and some slightly unusual ones (nothing very creative). The ice cream was reasonably creamy and while most of the flavors were good, some tasted clearly like artificial flavoring/syrups had been used heavily. 

For a home made ice cream shop, they carry an amazing variety of frozen yogurt flavors, including their attempt at the tart (awful) pinkberry imitation flavors. They all seemed to be very low calorie and while neither their flavors or the textures were standout, this place might be the very best destination for someone dieting and craving frozen yogurt, just based on their variety.

Categories: Steakhouses, Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: Came here a bunch the past couple years while at UCSB and noticed I never gave them a review. You want budget cheap sushi sushi? Come here. 1/2 off rolls and sushi all day every day. Recommend the Santa Barbara & Catalina rolls.

Categories: Korean, Restaurants | Review: Every visit is different and that's not a good thing.

The service has gone from slow to almost none existent with the worst being a We 12:15 arrival and it was 1:10 before food made it to the table. The latest visit was much better on the service side with meats grilling about 12 minutes after we hit the door. We were the only quests so I would expect fantastic service. We received good service. 

I worry about cleanliness. On a recent visit, our waiter refilled our drinks but when removing them from the table, grabbed close to the rim so whatever is on his hands is now in our mouths. Gross! You're servic raw pork and chicken and now grabbing my glass. I hope you washed your hands. 

Now even with all the negatives, I still go back...about once every 6-8 weeks for a long lunch. We can't get in and out in under 1.5hrs but sometimes you need that break. Should you go? Yes. Give it a shot as it's fun to share the cooking with friends. More fun than fondue.

Categories: Nightlife, Seafood, American (Traditional), Bars, Restaurants | Review: Food was good, 'Burnt Ends' sandwich very tasty and also reasonably fast. Waitress was very good and quick to refill soft drinks. Well priced also with good surroundings and great view looking back to the beach.

Loses a star as we were given our check well before we'd finished our food so felt a bit rushed out and one of the waiters was chasing pigeons out and decided to chase them straight towards us, nearly flying into us and our plates.

Categories: Wine Tasting Room, Local Flavor, Food, Wineries, Arts & Entertainment | Review: This review is for the wine club only. 

I have never encountered a more incompetent wine manager in my life. I am a member of multiple wine clubs, and this is by far the worst manager I have ever dealt with. I have been trying to have a fairly minor issue resolved for over 2 months now, and the manager continues to ignore me. I am not sure why such a simple thing is taking so long to resolve. Basic customer service is, if you say you're going to do something, do it. 

I will say, the wines here are great, so if you want to go in and taste, go for it, but whatever you do, do NOT join the wine club. If I am having this many problems with a small issue, I can't even begin to imagine what a nightmare it would be if there was any major issue.

Categories: Latin American, Restaurants, Mexican, New Mexican Cuisine | Review: Always absolutely delicious! This restaurant is part of the highest-rated Mexican restaurant group, Los Agvas, whose first location is still cooking on Milpas. Santo Mexcal is more upscale and nicer. Highly recommended

Categories: Steakhouses, Cafes, American (Traditional), Seafood, Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, Sandwiches | Review: Good food, great drinks. Service is good, prices are decent. 

Don't make the mistake of going for a late dinner on a Friday or Saturday. They start removing tables and the conversion from casual restaurant to hipster club begins.

Highly recommended if you're wanting cheap and strong drinks with comfort food to wash them down. On the other hand, if you're looking for great food you'll be disappointed.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: What Happened to this place????
I have not been here in a year and could not believe how downhill its gone.
Food was boring. Place was empty. Waiters were snobby...and its still too expensive!  Never going back.

Categories: Bars, American (New), Tapas Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife | Review: This place is wonderful! Everything on their food menu is exceptional and the drink menu tops most bars in Santa Barbara. The ginger coconut mojito is worth trying, but if you you don't want to commit to a whole drink they make sample drinks! Sample drinks-you can't beat that. The service is on point and super friendly too. Thanks Nectar! Highly recommend checking this place out.

Categories: Wine Bars, Bars, Beer Bar, Nightlife | Review: I really enjoyed the laid-back, rustic vibe of this wine bar. My friends and I had great service, drinking sangrias & wine while playing Scrabble. It was just good Sunday fun!

Categories: Irish Pub, Nightlife, Bars, Pubs | Review: Great pub with a lively atmosphere with various bands playing on certain nights and free peanuts in a big ass barrel by the front entrance :)

The scene here is not really a college clubbing atmosphere at all but a mature bar-hopping experience and a great place to start a Friday night. It can get crowded at times but everyone is pretty sociable and they have cool games like darts lol

Just don't wear flip flops because the peanut shells and whatnot get all up in your feet :(

Categories: Restaurants, Pizza | Review: Food is exceptional I have heard so many good things about this place. Finally made it to this pizzeria. All I can say is Wow . Staff is very friendly and great food

Categories: Indian, Restaurants | Review: $11 dollar lunch buffet -- pretty good variety of food but not amazing. It was very crowded and the tables were very close together. The naan arrived cold. Although there is more seating, I would recommend Flavor of India or Tamira's lunch buffets. The food is of superior quality for the same price. Still, pretty good for a fast lunch.

Categories: Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Tapas Bars, Spanish, Modern European, Bars, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Venues & Event Spaces, Restaurants | Review: A little bit of Spain in Santa Barbara. Who knew G&T's were the cocktail to enjoy with tapas. Had amazingly delicious home cured olives, grilled octopus, white asparagus and seafood paella. Everything was delicious. Service was only downside1-very crowded and we waited a long time for service. But was a lot of fun and would definitely return.

Categories: Asian Fusion, Seafood, Restaurants, Poke, Food | Review: Great Poke place! Great place to get lots of wins on the smash machine. Recommend the spam q - it tasted great. The slushes also amazing!!! 10/10

Categories: Modern European, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, Bakeries, Wine Bars, Food, Scandinavian, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Let me start off by saying that Alex is an amazing server. We came to brunch here with my kids and siblings on New Years Eve after a 2 hour drive. 
I am glad to say that my family and I were extremely satisfied. Food and service was great!!

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: The view, the view, the view. That pretty much sums it up for this restaurant. It is literally right on the beach, so just about every seat in the place has a spectacular view. 
We came here for breakfast from a recommendation and had to wait about 30-45 minutes for a table as it was crazy crowded. That was ok as we just took a nice stroll on the beach!
Here's where the 3 stars comes in. I totally understand it was crowded but it took forever for our waitress to finally stop by and even order out drinks. Our food took just as long to get-again, probably because it was really crowded.  I ordered the banana pancakes with fruit. The pancakes were fluffy and soft, very tasty. the coffee was just regular coffee, nothing too spectacular. 
If you don't mind the wait, this is definitely one of those local favorites to try while in SB.

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese | Review: The Chef was not wearing a mask, and was walking around through the entire restaurant.  We could see him in the kitchen cooking without it, and then he also walked right through the payment area mask-free. 

It took nearly an hour to get our takeout.  The cashier woman was nice, apologized, and gave us something extra to try and make up for it.  The wait wouldn't have been as much of an issue if all the food came at once, but it was one item out of five that we waited the majority of the time for, while the rest of the food grew cold.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Yelp! did us a solid by turning us on to this little gem of a restaurant.  The food is authentic, the portions are generous, the atmosphere is casual, and the service is friendly.  The salsa bar is loaded with fresh salsa selections.  If you are allergic to spice, go for the nice mild avocado salsa, and stay far, far away from the habanero salsa, which caused my taste buds to burn (but in a good way).  Street parking is bountiful.  Insider tip:  Hit Jaffurs winery down the street either before or after your meal and say hello to the boys (Craig, Dave and Matt) who run the joint!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Very popular with the locals.  A Julia Child favorite.  Usually a line out the door.  Very inexpensive, picnic table kinda place.  Food is good, but IMO not terrific.

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: Just went for the first time and it was very good.  The wine list is extensive and a bit pricey.  The food was so good and the ambiance was great. I would go back for sure. The French onion soup was so good.

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: This is a great quaint restaurant! The staff was very kind, the patio we ate on was well maintained and clean. The only issue is that we requested to have no sesame seeds on our meal, and we had sesame seeds on our food...  Other than that I recommend eating here!

Categories: Sandwiches, Restaurants, Delis, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: Their sandwiches and the other food items I have tried are great.  The only minus is their espresso.  It's not well prepared and, on top of that, it's served in big, disposable cups. With great coffee served in proper cups, this place would have been close to perfect.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Fish & Chips, Nightlife, Seafood, Restaurants, Bars | Review: Average touristy restaurant. Ordered clam chowder, cioppino, ahi and calamari. Best plate was clam chowder. Everything else was bland. Interesting view of harbor especially watching boats coming in and out with their daily catch. With so many outstanding restaurants in Santa Barbara probably won't return.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: Almost gave them 4 stars but the funny Asian bar tender in the back was awesome. Great music great vibe. Bottle service girls were cute too

Categories: American (New), Restaurants | Review: What an amazing culinary experience! The food was beyond phenomenal, as was the service. The night consisted of three acts: sea, land and sweets. The way that we were guided through each act was a special treat. The pairings of the wines and spirits were spot on as well. The choreography of the plating of each dish with the background soundtrack was quite something and made for some epic moments. 

This place is expensive but honestly worth every penny. If you can afford it, GO! You will not be disappointed. You'll be amazed. Get ready for something truly special. 

Thank you chefs for the best meal of my life!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: This place deserves another star for their happy hour! I tend to forget about this place since it's located away from the usual places you tend to think of when it comes to happy hour. The service was great this time and our waiter was very attentive. 
For a party of 3 and 4 happy hour drinks later with 2 appetizers ordered, free chips & salsa, our bill came out to $32 bucks! You can't beat that price. It's always nice to sit on the patio outside, especially when the weather is warm, but if you happen to go during the evening on a cool night they do have outside heaters that keep you warm.

Give this place a try if you haven't already!
Categories: Restaurants, Chinese, Fast Food | Review: Andrew litty made fire bowl four me and he's such a good worker he made me feel so happy and his sweet eyes made me get butterflies

Categories: Japanese, Restaurants, Noodles, Sushi Bars | Review: This is Yume sushi relocated! This is hands down my favorite sushi place - ever. Their fish is so fresh and flavorful and all of the employees are so nice and friendly. We love sashimi and sushi most, and you will not be disappointed. The sea scallop sushi comes with a hint of lemon, sea salt, and wasabi that accentuates the flavor so well. Other favorites are their yellow tail sashimi, albacore sushi, and sweet shrimp sushi. If you prefer rolls, they have you covered there too! My favorite is a creative roll called the Mike Dancing Pants named after a regular customer. On the appetizer side they have these "chili poppers" which are jalapeño poppers stuffed with crab, spicy tuna, and cream cheese lightly fried in tempura batter. So creative and delicious! We are so glad this sushi place was able to relocate to this awesome new location close to state street and the Granada instead of close down.

Categories: American (Traditional), Bars, Restaurants, Nightlife | Review: This place is solidly ok. The food is good, but not amazing. Walking in, I was very excited by the midwestern/southern feel, but between the food and service, I was not blown away. 

I've been here a couple of times. The first was with a large group. They were not happy with a large group and gave away one of our tables before our whole group got there. The service was slow and not particularly friendly. They have perfectly decent greasy food, but it is nothing to write home about.

Categories: Bubble Tea, Juice Bars & Smoothies, Food | Review: I love this place! it has only been here for a few months and is quite amazing. There is such a good option for types of tea, flavors and even the tapioca! Nice environment and the employees are so nice and welcoming. Nice location and easy to get to! overall really good place!!

Categories: Sushi Bars, Restaurants, Japanese | Review: HORRIBLE service. Came here at 8:30, ordered and gave a 15% tip in advance because that's how they close out the sale. Then they say my order has to be to go because we can't eat indoors after 8:30pm, even though they close at 9pm. So I asked if we can eat outside at the tables and they said yes. Then while I was still masked, I got my drink at the soda fountain and sat at a table to wait for my changes "to-go" order. I started drinking my drink without my mask on, since I cannot drink with it on. Other patrons who ordered before 8:30pm are eating and drinking their food and drinks without masks. I was singled out by the short-haired cashier/order taker for taking my mask off and drinking my drink while waiting for my "to-go" order. After I received my food, I told the rude short-haired cashier/order-taker that it doesn't hurt to be nice to your patrons. She singled me out while other patrons were eating and drinking without masks, she scolded me in front of my kids, and they made a scene in front of all the guests. NEVER again will I come to this establishment that DOES NOT CARE about their customers. I told the workers my tip goes to everyone who prepared my food, and the short haired rude cashier deserves a class on customer service.

Categories: Mexican, Bars, Tex-Mex, Nightlife, Dance Clubs, Sports Bars, Restaurants | Review: This bar is so fun!
I was here the weekend before July 4th with my friend. 

Drinks were made well: not too strong & not too sweet. They were playing hip hop music most of the night and almost everyone in the whole bar was dancing. They had a few beach balls being bounced around the dance floor. For part of the night they even had someone going around offering people free shots. How could I turn that down?

I'm not sure if they always do this or it was just for the holiday weekend because I'm not from the area. But next time I'm back in Santa Barbara, I'll definitely be back.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Lounges, Cocktail Bars, Mediterranean, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Nightlife | Review: Great atmosphere, comfortable and welcoming.   Friendly staff, quick service and the food was delicious.  I ordered the The BB Smash Burger:  angus patty, caramelized onions, American cheese, pickle aioli, tomato on a  brioche bun.  If you want fries they are a separate order.  Other menu options are the Seared White Sea Bass, Wagyu Hanger Steak and the House Made Ricotta Gnudi and additional Bites.

Nice dining experience for adults.  If you need to feed children you are out of luck because they don't have a kids menu.  On our visit we asked if they could make a grill cheese sandwich for my granddaughter which they did with a smile but the cost was the same as the Smash Burger.    It would/could be a great dining experience for a family if a kids menu was added.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Damn Gina you did it right... 

This taco spot is where your Tia from Mexico would come and get down on some Bombay tacos.. Love this spot. To bad I'm from Los Angeles and can't come more often...

Categories: Bars, Seafood, Food, Cocktail Bars, Steakhouses, Nightlife, Restaurants | Review: Fantastic place for brunch. The Eggs Benedict was fantastic and the hollandaise sauce was some of the best I've ever had. My wife had the seafood omelet with lobster sauce and was extremely impressed. The service was great, but it took a little long to get our food. However, that is my only complaint!

Categories: Food, Bakeries, Restaurants, Patisserie/Cake Shop, Cafes, Coffee & Tea | Review: Went here twice while on vacation with my mom. This is a great spot for a yummy, filling breakfast. Beautiful view of the water while you eat. The oat milk latte is one of the best I've ever had. The eggs, omelettes and pancakes are all really tasty as well.

Categories: Restaurants, Asian Fusion, Food, Poke, Hawaiian, Seafood | Review: G A S, but just a little pricey for poke. $18 in total for a medium bowl.

Best poke in the Goleta area

Categories: Restaurants, Food, Breakfast & Brunch, Bakeries | Review: I am now an official fan of the Egg's Rose.  After hearing about it on Food Network and through Yelp, I had to try it, since I love a salty breakfast.  It's a generous portion - very filling.  Casey had the two poached eggs on levain bread with preserves.  He really liked the preserves.

The service is a little slow, but I did get refills of coffee a couple times without asking.  A little pricey, we paid around $30 with tax/tip for our two entrees, a coffee, and a cafe au lait.  Will probably return to get another Egg's Rose and maybe try an omelet!

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Our experience was extremely disappointing with management. Upon entering the restaurant, we were greeted by a lovely young lady, first day on the job. We were unsure where we wanted to sit but decided on a spot that apparently, we were not allowed to sit in. The manager abruptly told the hostess that she could not sit us there because it was reserved for larger parties to consolidate. The way it was done was rude to the hostess and to us as patrons who could have been addressed directly out of courtesy. We were seated but left without ordering having lost our appetite. Bad energy cannot serve good food.

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Venues & Event Spaces, Peruvian, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Nightlife, Latin American, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Tapas/Small Plates, Mexican | Review: Incredible. This is the place to take that those out of town guests you want to impress, or yourself if you love yourself the way you should.

Our bartender, PJ was one of several staff who impressed us in our quick 50 minute pre-show drinks and appetizers experience. The service was personal, friendly and prompt without being obsequious or incessant. The chef came out when we asked about a gluten free option for the tempting taco appetizer and came up with a delicious lettuce wrap. We were sent a sample of honeycomb crisp when we expressed curiosity about it being "made in-house".

In other words, we felt as though each staff member we interacted with was personally committed to making our experience as pleasant as possible. Not only committed, but that they were actually enjoying seeing us enjoy ourselves. The food was delicious and creative, the drinks well made (compliments coming from a former chef and bartender). I look forward to my next occasion at this venue.

Categories: Restaurants, Thai | Review: This was some of the best Thai food I've ever had - and I've had a lot! The prices are great. Love that they have specials all day. I got the special combination Pad See Ew. You get a choice of soup or salad. The Tom Kha soup was the best I've ever had - so flavorful. It comes also comes with a spring roll, fried shrimp and wonton chips as well as rice and your entree. So much food for under $20! I will definitely return next time I find myself in Carpinteria.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I ordered two veggie tacos. They were delicious, but one of them has 2 large chunks of meat. I'm a vegetarian so you can imagine why this is a big problem for me. Thankfully I didn't eat the meat and caught it as soon as I bit into the taco because it wasn't easily visible in the taco. Inspect your tacos because they clearly are not careful about how they make their food here.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: Loved it! Ordering was a bit confusing because didn't get a menu until there was one person ahead of us, so felt a little rushed. Ordered the salmon/shrimp dish and it was amazing. It was on the sweeter side and flavors complemented each other but if you are not a fan on sweeter type of food, I wouldn't recommend it. Ordered the chipotle shrimp and fish burrito and it was good as well. The fresh chips were great. Loved that it wasn't salty.

Categories: Lounges, Venues & Event Spaces, Bars, Nightlife, Event Planning & Services, Dance Clubs | Review: My absolute favorite place to party in Santa Barbara when I'm in town, and has been for years. 

Also, some of the greatest people work there (Yeah Nelson, I mean you! You keep me coming back buddy.). People who really go the extra mile to make sure the night is memorable and amazing.

Thanks guys!

Categories: Seafood, Cajun/Creole, American (New), Restaurants | Review: Hmm. Sometimes without expecting it, perfection strikes...although I have never experienced in the form of popcorn crawfish until tonight. No reservation, 6:45 on a Friday night, table front corner...how does that happen? Great server. Perfect recommendations. Get the lamb chops or the scallops. A sing-a-long and magician. Who could ask for more? Will be back.

Categories: Pop-Up Restaurants, Korean, Restaurants, Asian Fusion | Review: Thank the Lord we got another different Asian fusion restaurant out here! I was seriously deprived of the variety of Asian food out here in SB, and then came along Secret Bao. The owners were the previous chefs from Loquita, so I knew it was going to be a treat and of course I was spot on. The shrimp bao is my all time favorite; the shrimps are nice and plump and perfectly fried. I usually ask for the slaw on the side because it's a mouthful. The pork belly was my 2nd favorite; it reminded me of an al pastor taco since there's pineapple in it. The fried chicken could be a tad spicier and crispier, but still good.

Categories: Delis, Gluten-Free, Fast Food, Salad, Sandwiches, Restaurants | Review: The chipotle turkey club was very very good. Definitely recommend. The pastrami melt was full
