of flavor. We also love the owner, Mark, he is an awesome guy. Thank you for wonderful sandwiches and great hospitality, Mark!

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Seafood | Review: The yelp reviews are very misleading. I got the carne asada burrito and my boyfriend got the shrimp burrito. My boyfriend had to ask for beans in the burrito because it only came with shrimp, vegetables, and rice. They were both incredibly bland and made both of our stomachs upset shortly after. Go somewhere else for Mexican food in SB.

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Restaurants, Bars, Tapas/Small Plates, American (New), Lounges, Seafood, Spanish, Italian, Nightlife, Mediterranean | Review: Went to Cadiz with a few friends last Saturday night for late night drinks and appetizers. We ordered every appetizer on the menu. My favorites were the home made gnocchi and spicy calamari. Two of us, regrettably, split a half dozen oysters on the half shell. We both ended up with with severe, violent debilitating food poisoning within 12 hours.   Therefore one star.  I think Cadiz must be Italian for diarrhea. Best part was I got to taste most of my appetizers twice!
Categories: Food, Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Desserts | Review: I wish I lived closer so I can take home a hand packed pint of Olive Oil and Toasted Almond. Omg....

Categories: Cocktail Bars, Lounges, Nightlife, Bars | Review: Such a cozy little bar with great drinks and speakeasy type of vibes! 

My girlfriend and I came on a trip to SB and she stumbled upon The Good Lion on yelp, so we walked right in around 3 pm on a Tuesday. Our bartender Harley was great! We loved how he paid such close attention to detail with every drink and he was super knowledgeable on drink recommendations. We were so shocked when he told us he had only been bartending for 2 months!

Not only was he a great bartender, but he offered tons of recommendations on places we should eat and drink!!! Not to mention, all the drinks were absolutely delicious!

We'll be returning again before we head home from our trip and can't wait to be back when we're in SB next!

Categories: Seafood, Specialty Food, Seafood Markets, Farmers Market, Restaurants, Food | Review: The Santa Barbara Fish Market is dishonest. They weren't upfront about certain up charges and then overcharged us for multiple items. 

We ordered 6 kushi oysters, a tray of uni, half pound of yellowtail, half pound of blue fin tuna, and half pound of salmon. 
 
First off, if you want to get your fish sliced, there is a 30% up charge. I understand if there is a fee, but that should have been disclosed from the start, and it wasn't. 

Second, as we were getting rung up, we got charged TWICE for the yellowtail, which is the most expensive at $24.95/lb. I'm not sure how this happened as the guy at the counter scanned two separate tickets, which means both tickets were for yellowtail. When I brought this to his attention, he went and rang up the proper item which is for the blue fin tuna. Blue Fin Tuna was $11.95/lb that day. We got half a pound so even with the up charge to get it sliced, it shouldn't have cost more than $7.75 - but they charged us $14.79, which equals $25.95/lb. When the manager came to help us, she said that's the correct price because we got the blue fin "loin." What? We never specifically asked for the "loin." We asked for what was displayed, so why did we get a different item than what we had asked for and if you have a different price for the "loin," why isn't that disclosed?

This business is dishonest and they charge people whatever price they want instead of the price displayed. It's such a tourist trap. If you want fresh seafood, check out Brophy Brothers or Santa Barbara Shellfish. They both offer really good / fresh seafood without the dishonesty. 

All in all, we spent $80 for everything and we didn't get that much food. For that price, we could have gotten just as much (if not more) food at a Japanese restaurant, and with much better service.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Food, Desserts, Japanese | Review: I was in Santa Barbara on vacation and we decided to grab some late night soosh. Yume is a cute sushi restaurant with ample seating in a less than busy part of downtown.

Ordered the Lion King roll and the Hurricane roll. We had originally ordered a different roll but they ran out of fish. It was like 8pm on Sunday ya'll.

The rolls here are on the bigger side and definitely catered to more western palates. While the sushi was tasty, it didn't light my world on fire.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch | Review: I'm from LA so I know what authentic Mexican food taste like. I went in today for the first time ever and had some Menudo, it was soooooo good!!! Best place in SB county for sure! The owner is from Guerrero so it has that home made flavor that most surrounding restaurants lack. Would love to give 10 stars instead of 5, I feel like I'm short changing them. Very friendly and down to earth staff. I felt like I was having lunch at my Tia's house. If you're in the area you have to definitely stop by and check this place out. I'm coming back tomorrow just to try the pozole.

Categories: Soup, Thai, Restaurants, Noodles | Review: Meh. I ordered the fried fish with chili and got some thick-battered half-fried fish of some sort with bits and pieces of bell pepper, canned button mushrooms, and canned bamboo shoots. Nothing hits the spot like canned vegetables, right? It also came with an egg roll and some fried wontons which I thought was kinda weird. The fried wontons were alright but the egg roll I could not finish. Ugh... I've never had thai like this before and I hope I never do again.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: 0 Zero.. if I could do it.. Food Quality is horrible, and very expensive.. just drunk people come here.. so they think the food is good .. Worst Margaritas I had ever had in my life.. Don't go here.. save your money and go to Mc Donald's.. Kid Prices are a joke too.. Don't go just Don't

Categories: Seafood, Bars, Wine Bars, American (New), Restaurants, French, Nightlife, Mexican | Review: We went to Bouchon because it was voted best restaurant for 2012 on santabarbara.com. If I were to describe the food with one word it would be- fresh. I got the special tomato salad to start and liked my salad more than my main chicken entrée. Overall it was expensive ($25 for chicken), but when everything tastes so fresh, it's worth it. Even the bread was amazing. Our service was good (excellent recommendations) but at times slow. Definitely make reservations.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars, Japanese | Review: Edomasa has become my go-to place for sushi rolls. They have good prices and a varied menu. I usually get Tokyo, Marina, and Hip-Hop roll and is a good amount of food for two. My husband has also tried their beef ramen and it was super tasty. 

Friendly staff and a perfect option for a Friday night dinner!

Categories: Restaurants, Sandwiches, American (New) | Review: We visited my son and took my hubby out for his birthday. Unfortunately his birthday fell on Mother's Day in 2012 but I called during the week, and they were happy to take my reservation.

This time the dining area was full up. My son noticed a nobel laureate seated outside in the patio dining area. I noticed a noisy but fun loving table next to us with what appeared to be drama or theater students and their professors seated next to a family with very young kids up to what might have been grandparents or great grandparents.  
Point being a diverse group of diners, and full room but the service was fine and responsive.

Categories: Restaurants, Japanese, Ramen | Review: This is near where I work, and I went just after lunch on a weekday.  No line and I sat at the bar.  I ordered the original tonkatsu with thick noodles, pork belly & a soft-boiled egg.  Quite frankly, I was disappointed.  The first bowl they brought me had thin instead of thick noodles, so I sent it back.  When the second bowl came, the noodles were thicker than the first bowl, but I wouldn't describe them as "thick" - I would call them more "regular" ramen noodles.  The soup was just mediocre.  Not particularly flavorful and not very high on my umami scale. I was not given any chili oil or any other spices or condiments and none were on the bar.  The noodles were the most disappointing, they were not chewy and did not help to add body to the lackluster soup.  When the menu said "SB Craft noodles" I guess I set my expectations too high.  The pork belly was OK, again, nothing special.  It seemed bland and not particularly tender.  It would not break apart with chopsticks, I had to pick the entire piece up and bite-off a hunk.  The egg was good, the yolk was not over cooked, although the white was a bit rubbery.  The only other things in the soup were one piece of nori and a giant pile of onions.  I *might* go back to try the dipping noodles, but that would be the only reason.

Categories: Restaurants, Sushi Bars | Review: By far the worst place I have ever been too!! I would've loved to give negative stars. To start off, when we first got into the restaurant we were. It greeted or asked to sit anywhere and we ended up seating ourselves. Then to make matters even worst it took them 20 minutes to realize we didn't have menus or drinks yet. We put in our orders for some pretty simple food items. The first waiter was not accurate with our order and messed our drink orders up and who knows when he put our rolls in because we didn't receive them until we had to persistently ask for them which took about 45 minutes. Soon after we put in our meal order and that came out 20 minutes later which was fine thanks to the lady who waited on us after we called for her because no one was helping us, which by the way I wish I asked for her name because she was the only helpful one and got our order and meals out at a reasonable time. Our first waiter disappeared on us literally like went M.I.A. But the overall experience was the worst ever!! The male waiters just stood around and didn't even bother coming to ask how everything was or if we needed anything, but the female waitress was by far very helpful and respectful. To sum everything up our food came an hour after we had put in our orders while people around us were eating, enjoying their meals even though we came before! At the end of our meal the first male waiter finally returned out of the nowhere and gave us the bill for which he didn't even have any business in because he left us hanging. He had our bill completely messed up because he had no idea what we ordered and was going to charge us for items we did not receive. PLAIN OUT BAD BAD BAD WORST SERVICE EVER NOT TO MENTION ITS A MONDAY NIGHT AND THE PLACE WAS NOT EVEN PACKED. The only excuse they had was that they were understaffed!! UNEXCEPTABLE!!! I will not be returning!

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I live in carp and this is one of my all time favorite places, but I only like one thing on the menu. It's called the monte alban, and it's a creamy garlicky chicken zucchini dish over rice that is absolutely to die for! I get it with mushrooms and extra sauce. With the free chips and salsa it can feed 2.

Categories: Asian Fusion, Poke, Seafood, Food, Restaurants | Review: Has to be my fav Poke place in SB so far! The service is also really great and the workers are always so friendly and kind. I got a poke bowl with salmon, tuna, and spicy tuna. Next time I don't think      I'll get the spicy tuna, I feel like it clashed too much with the rest of my bowl, but it was okay. I tried all of their sauces except the super spicy one because I don't got time for that, but it was actually really good! It is a little pricey but I mean we are in SB so... but overall really good!

Categories: Restaurants, Chinese | Review: Although I have since moved on, this was the only good Chinese I could find in the Goleta area, and I do miss it so. It was hard to not feel like you were being treated like family there (including the hugs while leaving!). The menu was short, focused, and good. They didn't deliver to the Ellwood area, but it was worth the drive.

Categories: Restaurants, Vegetarian, Vegan | Review: Forget the haters who leave 1 star reviews bitching and whining that gourmet organic food costs more than junk food. This is a great restaurant. Go back to Subway or Taco Bell.

My wife and I travel all over the world, were foodies even before going veg and we have eaten at all the top vegetarian and vegan restaurants in the USA and I can tell you without a doubt that Adama has some of the best food we've ever tasted. During our recent trip to SB we thought Adama was so good we went back 4 or 5 times and enjoyed lunches and dinners.

Most vegans would rank Millenium (San Francisco) and Sublime (Ft. Lauderdale) as the top vegan restaurants in the country but we found as good (and better) dishes at Adama and guess what....at much lower prices!

Maybe it's not as fancy or "upscale" as either of those two but the prices are much more reasonable. Quality, fresh, organic ingredients do not come cheap and Adama offers a great value for the quality of their dishes. 

Their dinner entrees range from $14 to $20 with sides! Compare this to ANY decent/nice restaurant in Santa Barbara or anywhere else and that is not expensive. This is a nice restaurant with a warm setting in an old home that has been renovated with eclectic decor. The service was always good and the staff make you feel like part of the family. We got to know the manager and owner during our visits there and they seemed like old friends. They are both very knowledgable and dedicated to their craft and the owner/chef has created recipes that are creative, hearty, healthy and delicious.

Speaking of the food, the salad greens are locally sourced and I'm not normally a salad guy but the greens were so fresh and flavorful that I ordered salads with every meal. I recommed the garlic peppercorn dressing. Yummmm!

I'm a gourmet sandwich guy and when I almost peed myself when I saw veg versions of French Dip, Barbeque Sandwich, Meatball Sub, Patty Melt and others. The proteins were epic and I highly recommed all these. The patty melt was the best sandwich I've had in a long time.

For dinner I recommend the Gardein Picatta (tastes like chicken) or the "Vegan KFC" (tastes like southern fried chicken). Those dishes rocked! My wife also loves the veggie skillet.

Geez, I almost forgot...They make their own bakery items and have guilt free muffins, cupcakes, cakes and pies that were all tasty and homemade. The zuchinni bread was to die for.

PLEASE open Adama 2 in Orange County! Cant wait to come back to SB and eat at Adama!!!

Categories: Pakistani, Restaurants, Buffets, Seafood, Halal, Indian, Vegetarian, Vegan, Food, Salad, Desserts | Review: Had dinner with my recent college grad son and two of his friends from school.  We all agreed that the chicken tikka, saag Paneer, and chana we all carefully prepared and tasty.  The garlic naan Be careful in asking for extra spicy.  Medium hot is still plenty.  Prices are very reasonable.  We will be back.

Categories: Vietnamese, Restaurants, Bars, Seafood, Wine Bars, Vegetarian, Nightlife, Noodles | Review: First time in this place! 
It look small from the outside but once you see the place inside is very spacious and it has a lot of tables and chairs.
When we walk in there's this guy who helped us get a table and gave us menu right away.
Then our server came in and she was very nice and patient to us. Since it's our first time we asked her for any recommendations and she said chicken curry and rice, and for my friend 74D (sorry I forgot the name of the food) so that's what we got.
The chicken curry has a lot of soup but small portion of meat and that's the same with my friend's order.
The food is good but it's a bit pricey.

Categories: Venues & Event Spaces, Cafes, Wine Bars, Event Planning & Services, Nightlife, Bars, Restaurants, American (New) | Review: I had my Wedding dinner/reception here, and Jennifer was exceptionally professional, kind and accommodating. We changed our wedding venue a few months ago to the Wine Cask so I was glad that we found this venue to host our event. 

My DJ was received with ease and put in the perfect spot. My wedding helpers were allowed in early to do a drop off with my special items. They perfectly set up my cake and decorations:  flower arrangements were placed on the tables. It was so smooth and effortless, when guests arrived they were greeted with drinks and appetizers. 

We thoroughly enjoyed our selves, my husband was very pleased with the five star service the staff provided. They provided impeccable waitstaff and bartender to work our event. The Wine Cask exceeded our expectations with ceaseless attention to us and our guests. Food and drink was optimum, coming out hot and fast, after my guests placed their orders. We had a party of forty in the library room and lounging in in the courtyard. My guest were lively and complimentary by the venue we chose. I will definitely come back to enjoy a romantic dinner with my husband come anniversary time.

Categories: International Grocery, Imported Food, Grocery, Ethnic Food, Specialty Food, Food | Review: They have everything you need at this Japanese Market and do not have to drive to LA.

The staff are super friendly and helpful at reading labels.

It is more expensive, but you are saving the drive.

There is so much cramed into this store.

I love the bean curd pockets :P

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Great hole-in-the-wall cheap Mexican food. It's a little difficult finding the place because it is on the backside of the shopping center. I was immediately greeted once I walked in and when it took me awhile to order they were really helpful with making my selection. (Large burrito on the run) Once I got my food I noticed the burrito was a little small to be large. When I approached the register they asked what was wrong and immediately fixed the situation and apologized. They even gave me a free container of salsa for the inconvenience! Overall, I had a great friendly experience. I almost gave it 4 stars because the place is pretty run down (using I flimsy screen door as one of their entrances, but it wouldn't be a hole-in-the-wall if it was like this which I enjoy!
Categories: Bagels, Restaurants, Breakfast & Brunch, Food | Review: Pretty good deals and bagels, the decaf coffee is kinda weak...

Categories: Ice Cream & Frozen Yogurt, Event Planning & Services, Food, Caterers | Review: The ice cream is very delicious but SO expensive. The tiniest cup had about 3 tablespoons of ice cream for $2.50

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: Went in on Tues for tacos.  Saw the bill board outside which said asada tacos also avail at sale price on Mondays. $1.25@  No carnitas my go to so I ordered 1 asada,  and 2 al pastor.   The tacos are small... palm of your hand size w/ 1 tortilla but fair quantity of meat.  Salsa bar was good with cilantro, radishes, hot red, medium hot salsa,  green salsa... that's all I remember. There is a full menu of other platos y combinaciones.  There are bottled drinks but I chose Horchata... large was $3.25 whereas Cuca's costs $2.50 for the same size.  Both were good.   Staff was helpful and polite.

Categories: Wine Tasting Room, Wineries, Food, Arts & Entertainment | Review: Lovely tasting room located in the newly renovated lower State St area, just feet away from Stearns Wharf in one direction and The Funk Zone, in the other. It has a sleek modern interior with bar seating and small group table options. They're also dog friendly! I would recommend eating before or after your visit and trying the $15 flight.  

I primarily drink Cab Sav and was a little sad to see they did not carry any, however, the Pinot Noirs were just as pleasant as a Cab would've been, for me. My favorite of the flight was the 2016 Estate Pinot Noir, hands down, though all tastes were delicious. Individual glasses were a little pricey, in comparison to the rest of the Funk Zone, ranging $14-24 a glass. 

The staff were kind and attentive, moving a little slowly with a large crowd but very knowledgeable on their wine selection. There's a single restroom in the back and tons of parking around the area, though not right out front or back of the tasting room. It's a beautiful environment with high quality wines and I would definitely come back for a glass of the 2016 Pinot or to try their future blends.

Categories: Coffee & Tea, Food, Bakeries, Coffee Roasteries | Review: I love this local coffee shop! The coffee is always fresh and the owners are awesome and so friendly! I come here every week and I love the atmosphere and vibe. Great breakfast empanadas and americanos!

Categories: Nightlife, Dance Clubs | Review: Very racist and rude wouldn't recommend to foreign peoples to go there
 Would have given 0 star because one of the security is very sexist and disrespectful

Categories: Breakfast & Brunch, Restaurants, American (Traditional) | Review: While the view is lovely, I wouldn't recommend coming here for a good breakfast. My husband and I read some good reviews online but were very disappointed by the service, the food quality and the price of breakfast at this place. 

I was told that they could not add onions and garlic to my omelette because they were too busy. These were the exact words from the cashier. Then I ordered a bacon and egg omelette which was listed on the board as $10.95 but when we got the bill, we were charged an extra $3.25 for the bacon. 

I don't eat white potatoes or toast so I asked not to get those sides but my plate came out with the potatoes on it. I felt bad for wasting food but I had to. 

The price was $33 for two of us which included very old and burnt coffee for $5.50.

Needless to say, my husband and I won't ever go back there. Think twice about going there for breakfast. It seems to be a tourist trap.

Categories: Restaurants, Mexican | Review: I really liked this place... But feel like it's not as good as it use to be. The food has been luke warm, there's really not a whole lot prep or time going into the dishes... Bummed, when I first came here years ago I remember it being amazing! Upside, their chips and salsa are on point.

Categories: Mexican, Restaurants | Review: The tacos are inexpensive and delicious. They have a wide variety of meats to choose from, and sodas from mexico. Tongue is delicious, and marinated pork as well. THESE ARE REAL TACOS. Perfect after a run or yoga sesh.

Categories: Seafood, Restaurants, Hawaiian, American (New), Breakfast & Brunch | Review: We went here this past weekend.  We used to love The Brown Pelican and thought we would give this new company a try.  You sure can't be the locale! Gorgeous views and right on the beach.
The food was only marginally good.  When it was the Brown Pelican the food was excellent. The service was good then too.  Here we had to flag down our waitress just to get our iced tea refilled.
After waiting an hour for a table (after being told it would be 45 minutes) we missed the cut off for the Sunday brunch. The place and parking lot was a zoo.
The New England clam chowder was so bad I didn't finish it. I had the ceviche. Nothing special and not spicy. The chips tasted like they were grocery store chips.

Hubby had the poblano and mushroom tacos. He also said they had no kick to them and bland.

Sorry to say this is the last time we will be going there although it is where my husband and I went on our first date (to the Brown Pelican).
